title: Solidity Smart Contract Engineer -Multiliquid
location: US only (preference for onsite/hybrid in LA)

jobsdetail.description:
Description

About Uniform Labs


We are an on-chain financial infrastructure company backed by leading VCs including Strobe Fund, CMT Digital, Metalayer VC, The Venture Dept, and Generative Ventures. We're upgrading financial markets through 24/7 instant settlement. Our first product is Multiliquid, an institutional-grade smart contract protocol that facilitates interoperability and liquidity across tokenized financial assets and stablecoins. Our mission is to bridge traditional finance (TradFi) assets and decentralised finance (DeFi) in a transparent, efficient, and highly scalable way, ultimately replatforming all of financial markets on 10x better infrastructure in the form of blockchain.


Role Overview


We're looking for a Smart Contract Developer to join our core engineering team. In this hands-on role, you'll design, develop, and deploy secure smart contracts that power the Multiliquid protocol. You'll architect on-chain logic, optimize gas efficiency, ensure security best practices, and build the foundational infrastructure that enables institutional-grade DeFi operations. This is an opportunity to shape the core protocol layer of institutional DeFi, building robust, auditable smart contracts that handle significant liquidity flows across multiple chains.


You will work on a small, rigorously selected, senior team of engineers. You will be responsible for collaborating closely with product, compliance, security auditors, and business stakeholders. This is a high-impact role requiring deep technical expertise in blockchain development, strong security mindset, strategic thinking, and a passion for delivering best-in-class DeFi solutions.


Key Responsibilities

Smart Contract Development

Design, develop, test, and deploy secure smart contracts in Solidity for the Multiliquid protocol using Foundry.

Implement complex DeFi primitives including liquidity pools, cross-chain bridges, access control mechanisms, and institutional-grade financial logic.

Write clean, well-documented, gas-optimized code that adheres to industry best practices and established design patterns.


Protocol Architecture

Architect scalable, modular smart contract systems that support evolving protocol requirements.

Design upgradeable contract patterns and governance mechanisms for protocol evolution.

Collaborate with the engineering team to define on-chain/off-chain boundaries and integration points.


Security & Auditing

Conduct thorough internal security reviews and testing of smart contracts before deployment.

Work closely with external auditors to address findings and implement recommended security improvements.

Proactively identify and mitigate potential vulnerabilities, attack vectors, and edge cases.

Stay current with emerging security threats and best practices in the DeFi ecosystem.


Testing & Quality Assurance

Write comprehensive unit tests, integration tests, and fuzzing tests for all smart contract code.

Develop test suites that cover edge cases, failure scenarios, and adversarial conditions.

Participate in code reviews and provide constructive feedback to maintain high code quality standards.


Cross-Chain & Integration Work

Implement cross-chain messaging protocols and bridge integrations to enable multi-chain liquidity flows.

Collaborate with full-stack engineers to ensure seamless integration between smart contracts and front-end/back-end systems.

Design and document contract interfaces (ABIs) that enable efficient off-chain interaction.


Hands-On Engineering

Actively write and deploy smart contract code to mainnet and testnets.

Lead the development of core protocol features and upgrades.

Monitor deployed contracts, respond to on-chain events, and troubleshoot issues in production environments.

Prototype, iterate, and optimize rapidly in a fast-moving environment.
jobsdetail.benefits:
Why Join Us?

Impact: Help shape the user experience and interface of the future liquidity layer for tokenized finance.

Innovation: Work on real-world blockchain integrations powering institutional-grade capital flows.

Learning: Expand your understanding of DeFi, tokenized assets, and decentralized infrastructure.

Team: Join a collaborative, mission-driven team backed by top investors.

Upside: Competitive salary, equity upside, and the chance to be an early builder of a transformative protocol.


Compensation & Package

Base Salary + Equity

Interview Process

1.Founder / CEO Interview

2.Founder / CEO Interview

3.Technical Interview

4.Final Interview


About Multiliquid

Our first product is Multiliquid, an institutional-grade smart contract protocol that facilitates interoperability and liquidity across tokenized financial assets and stablecoins. Our mission is to bridge traditional finance (TradFi) assets and decentralized finance (DeFi) in a transparent, efficient, and highly scalable way, ultimately replatforming all of financial markets on 10x better infrastructure in the form of blockchain.

1-10

United States

M0 is a universal stablecoin platform. With M0, builders can create their own stablecoins by using M0’s platform, and customize attributes such as branding, compliance behaviors, and yield distribution mechanics. By leveraging M0, businesses can monetize their digital dollar use cases and maintain control over their money tech stack, while not being burdened by operational heavy-lifting.

Unlike traditional stablecoin models, M0 is built as an open multi-issuer framework that delivers seamless interoperability between $M and its extensions, but also allows builders of custom stablecoins to upgrade into their own issuers and not depend on anyone else for issuance.

M0 redefines the stablecoin landscape by eliminating traditional barriers to issuance, enabling an open and composable financial ecosystem where businesses and developers can create, customize, and scale their digital dollar solutions with unmatched flexibility. As a neutral and transparent protocol, M0 ensures that stablecoin liquidity remains fluid, accessible, and optimized for efficiency—laying the groundwork for a more decentralized and resilient financial system.

11-50


Headquarters

Germany

title: Senior Backend Engineer - Onramp ( BATON CORP)

jobsdetail.description:
Description

Location: New York, NY (Full-Time, In-Person)

Company: Baton Corporation


Who We Are


We’re Baton Corporation, a team of elite builders moving fast and redefining what’s possible.

We’re a high-growth software company and proud contributors to pump.fun, the world’s largest crypto social network. We build for the future.


What You’ll Do


As a Backend Engineer, you’ll design and scale the fintech and crypto systems powering seamless fiat ↔ crypto flows, real-time risk decisions, and rock-solid financial records.


You will:


  1. Own payment orchestration for bank rails (ACH, RTP, FedNow, SEPA, Open Banking) and cards (Apple Pay, Google Pay) with idempotent, fault-tolerant APIs and webhook processing.
  2. Integrate third-party providers (Plaid, TrueLayer, Tink, Stripe, Checkout.com, Adyen, Chainalysis, TRM, Travel Rule networks) behind clean, reliable interfaces.
  3. Build a real-time risk engine (<300ms) combining rules, ML scores, device signals, sanctions/KYC hits, and blockchain analytics to drive GREEN/AMBER/RED outcomes.
  4. Implement KYC/AML primitives (CIP/CDD, sanctions/PEP screening, Travel Rule data, audit-ready journaling) in close partnership with Compliance.
  5. Ship a double-entry ledger and reconciliation system tying together processors, bank statements, and on-chain balances with daily break detection and automated remediation.
  6. Deliver crypto to user wallets safely and fast (e.g., USDC/SOL), managing hot-wallet flow, transaction creation, confirmations, and operational safeguards.
  7. Raise the bar on reliability — SLOs, circuit breakers, retries, DLQs, shadow traffic, and canary releases.
  8. Instrument everything with traces, metrics, and logs. Build internal ops & compliance dashboards and participate in on-call for a 24/7 system.
  9. Collaborate cross-functionally with product, mobile, compliance, risk, and finance to take features from concept to production.
jobsdetail.benefits:

If you thrive in chaos, love ambitious problems, and want to build something game-changing, we want to hear from you.


At our core, we believe in:

  1. ⚡ Speed over perfection — ship now, iterate later.
  2. 🧠 Ownership over bureaucracy — everyone makes big decisions.
  3. 💎 Talent density over headcount — we hire the best, no exceptions.
  4. 🌍 Building for the future — crypto moves fast, and so do we.

title: Product Lead (Morpho Prime) -Morpho Labs

jobsdetail.description:
Description

Morpho is a leading Decentralized Finance (DeFi) lending protocol that recently secured $50 million in funding from Ribbit Capital, a16z crypto, Coinbase Ventures, Variant, Brevan Howard, Pantera, Blocktower, and 50 others to support its mission of revolutionizing lending with an open, efficient, and resilient platform. Morpho is experiencing explosive growth, with over $9 billion in assets supplied on the protocol, solidifying itself as the new standard for DeFi lending. Now, Morpho is scaling its team of contributors to establish itself as a cornerstone, not just of DeFi, but of a new internet-native financial system.


Location

Paris or remote (from GMT -5 up to GMT +2 to ensure sufficient overlap with the rest of the team).


Mission & Responsibilities

We’re looking for a Product Lead to drive Morpho’s Prime Products vertical.


You’ll work with engineering, design, data and protocol teams to set strategy, ship industry-shaping products and help Curators and Advanced Users interact with Morpho’s protocol.

To do so, you will need to develop a deep understanding of Morpho’s protocol and products, various stakeholders, the market, competitors and the wider Defi and lending industry as a whole.


Specifically, you will:

Understand Morpho’s various stakeholders, their needs and requirements,

Deeply understand the wider crypto lending space, both Centralized and Decentralized,

Execute on the product vision and strategy with a world-class engineering team,

Shape the products that Curators, Institutions and Pro users use to manage risk and exposure.
jobsdetail.requirement:
Must-have Experience & Skills

Job requirements

5+ years of experience building and shipping products in the Crypto Lending or Trading space,

Understanding of technical web development fundamentals (APIs, SDKs…),

Understanding of Risk Management and trade execution in crypto and DeFi,

Experience working with large institutions, funds or capital allocators in Defi or Finance,

Experience working directly with Engineering teams to deliver 0-to-1 products,

Exceptional organization and communication skills,

Low-ego, high-ownership mindset.
jobsdetail.benefits:

(Optional – cover note)


About Morpho Labs

Overview and Mission

Morpho Labs is the development company behind Morpho, a leading decentralized lending protocol. Its mission is to make financial infrastructure a public good by providing an open, efficient, and resilient platform for on-chain loans. Morpho enables anyone to earn yield or borrow crypto assets permissionlessly, while also allowing developers and businesses to build custom lending markets and vaults on its infrastructure.


Since launch, Morpho has grown into one of the largest DeFi lending systems (≈$6 B in deposits as of mid-2025) operating on Ethereum and other chains. Its vision is to become a cornerstone of a new internet-native financial system by fusing DeFi lending with traditional fintech.


Product Offerings

Morpho offers a suite of lending/borrowing products and developer tools built around its smart-contract protocol:


Consumer Products (Earn/Borrow):

Retail users can deposit assets into Morpho Vaults to earn optimized interest, or post collateral to borrow other tokens. Morpho optimizes yields by peer-to-peer matching within its vaults on top of base pools like Aave/Compound. Borrowers enjoy higher collateral limits and no protocol fees.


Curate/Vaults (Prime):

Power users and institutions can deploy custom vaults and lending markets. The Curate interface lets “vault curators” configure collateral types, interest models, and risk parameters, earning fees for supplying liquidity. These vaults are open, customizable, and scalable, benefiting from Morpho’s liquidity and matching engine. This is powered by the “Morpho Blue” protocol — a minimal core lending engine designed for security and flexibility.


Developer Tools (Infra):

Morpho provides on-chain and off-chain infrastructure for developers. A public GraphQL API and SDK libraries let applications query Morpho data and integrate lending functions. Enterprises can build their own lending use cases on Morpho’s stack, benefitting from audited, formally-verified contracts and DevOps tools.


Morpho infrastructure underpins notable integrations — for example, Coinbase’s on-chain lending launched in early 2025 uses Morpho on the Base chain to offer crypto-backed loans through the Coinbase app.


Funding History

Morpho Labs has raised approximately $70 M in venture funding (mostly through token sales):

Seed Round (Oct 2021): ~$1.35 M, led by Semantic Ventures and Nascent

Strategic Round (July 2022): $18 M, co-led by a16z Crypto and Variant

Strategic Round (Aug 2024): $50 M, led by Ribbit Capital with a16z, Coinbase Ventures, Pantera, Brevan Howard, BlockTower, Kraken Ventures, and others

These funds are supporting growth on Ethereum and expansion to new chains.


Team Structure and Leadership

Morpho Labs is structured with both for-profit and nonprofit entities, but engineering is centralized under the development company.

The engineering organization is divided into smart-contract and off-chain teams. Core protocol development is handled by a small group focused on Morpho Blue, vaults, and oracles. A separate infrastructure/API team manages backend services and the public API. The Head of Engineering will lead this off-chain group and help scale engineering practices.


Technology Stack and Culture

Morpho emphasizes security, minimalism, and modern DevOps:

On-chain: Solidity (Morpho Blue is ~600 LOC), fully audited, formally verified, $2.5M bug bounty

Off-chain: TypeScript (Node.js), Kubernetes, Helm, Terraform, GraphQL API

Dev Culture: Research-driven, collaborative, with open discussion and weekly “DeFi School” sessions

Engineers are encouraged to deeply threat-model and simplify systems. Most work is remote-friendly with periodic in-person bootcamps.

Recent Updates (Past 12 Months)

Corporate Restructuring: Morpho Labs SAS merged into the Morpho Association nonprofit; all equity transferred to the DAO.

Coinbase Partnership: Coinbase launched on-chain lending powered by Morpho on Base.

Token Unlock: MORPHO governance token became transferable in late 2024.

Growth: Deposits have scaled to ~$6 B; ongoing protocol upgrades and mobile features released.


Open Source and Community Contributions

Morpho’s contracts and tools are open source under a dual license. The GitHub organization hosts:

Morpho Blue (core protocol)

Vaults, oracles, and rewards contracts

TypeScript SDKs for integration

Developer documentation and tooling

They also regularly publish lending protocol research and offer one of the largest bug bounties in DeFi.

Locations and Remote Policy


Morpho Labs is based in Paris, with a remote-friendly culture. Team members work across Europe and North America (GMT-5 to GMT+2 time zones). Roles are open to remote engineers with occasional meetups at the Paris HQ.


Resources

Fundraise

https://www.theblock.co/post/308866/ribbit-capital-50-million-raise-morpho-labs

Dune

https://dune.com/morpho/gmorpho-dashboard

Morpho's Mission

Nansen - Guide to Morpho

https://www.nansen.ai/post/what-is-morpho

title: Software Engineer - Fullstack (Web3 dApp Development)- Sei Labs Inc
description:
Important: Please read the following before referring candidates

Must be able to do NYC 2-3 days a week. They're happy to look at backend focused or full stack. Not pure frontend.


Description

About Sei

Sei Labs builds open sourced technology for the high-performance Sei Blockchain, the first parallelized EVM Layer 1 blockchain designed to scale with the industry. The unique optimizations built into Sei enable Web3 applications to reach Web2 level scale and performance, driving the mass adoption of digital assets.


Our teams are comprised of former Google, Goldman Sachs, Robinhood, Nvidia, and Databricks veterans dedicated to onboarding the next billion users onto a vibrant ecosystem of applications.


Sei Labs has raised over $30M from top investors including Jump Crypto, Multicoin Capital, Coinbase Ventures, Distributed Global, Hudson River Trading, and Flow Traders.


We're a globally distributed team with a fully remote workforce. Check out our website and docs for more details.


Fullstack Engineering at Sei

Sei Labs is looking for a Fullstack Engineer to help shape the future of Web3 by empowering innovative, high-impact applications across the Sei Blockchain. You'll work with a team at the forefront of blockchain technology, collaborating closely with our ecosystem partners to develop cutting-edge dApps spanning diverse Web3 domains (from DEX’s and games, to DePin, AI and beyond). If you're passionate about constant learning, rapid development, and making a tangible impact in a fast-paced, community-first environment, this is a unique opportunity to push the boundaries of what's possible in decentralized technology.

jobsdetail.description:
About Hashgraph:


Hashgraph is a fast-growing software company committed to supporting, developing and servicing Hedera, an open source, proof-of-stake platform. Hedera is EVM-compatible and has been specifically built to meet the needs of enterprise and Web3 applications, which require speed, security, stability and sustainability. Hedera’s public network is governed by industry-leading organizations, spanning 11 sectors and 14 regions who oversee the development and direction of the decentralised platform.


About The Team:


The Execution Team is one of the core teams that builds and maintains the Hiero Consensus Node, the heart of the Hiero Distributed Ledger Technology (DLT). Our team is responsible for delivering the Hiero native services, which include the Crypto, Token, Consensus, and File Services. We also own transaction processing, fee collection, rewards accounting, and distribution. Furthermore, the generation of records and blocks is part of our responsibility. Our team works primarily in Java, and our code is open source. We collaborate closely with internal and external partners to deliver a reliable, performant, scalable, secure, fault-tolerant, and developer-friendly platform.


About the role:


As a Staff Software Engineer, you’ll be a technical leader on the Execution Team, responsible for delivering software services, business logic, and APIs within the Hiero Consensus Node. You’ll work on deeply technical challenges involving distributed execution, state management, transaction lifecycles, and economic systems (fees, rewards, and penalties). You will work in a highly collaborative environment where innovation, trust, and ownership are key values. This is a high-impact role requiring deep passion for building the platform that powers next-generation decentralised applications.


You may find yourself doing all of the following:

Design, develop, test and debug core components of the execution layer

Collaborate with cross-functional teams to define and deliver end-to-end functionality

Optimize performance and scalability of the execution layer for high-throughput transaction workloads

Ensure correctness, determinism, and reliability in service implementations

Drive high standards in code quality, testing, and documentation

Mentor engineers and contribute to a culture of technical excellence and open-source collaboration

Having a passion for code quality, system design, and doing things the right way

Other skills that are great to bring with you but that we can help you develop:

Experience in distributed ledger Technologies (DLT) or blockchain platforms

Exposure to security principles in distributed applications or cryptographic systems

Familiarity with web3 tools or smart contract platforms (especially EVM)

Experience with event-driven architectures or high-frequency trading systems

Description

Who We Are:

We build infrastructure that delivers massive amounts of web data to the companies training the world’s most powerful AI models.

We're the team that helps to power and support Grass, a bandwidth-sharing network that lets us operate a massive distributed crawler, giving us unique access to high-quality public web data at global scale. On top of that, we’ve built pipelines for ingesting, segmenting, and annotating billions of videos, transcripts, and audio files, powering dataset creation for frontier labs.

We’re lean, technical, and move fast. No red tape, no slow decision-making; just a team of builders pushing to expand what’s possible for open web data and AI.


The Role.

We are looking for a Senior Software Engineer (Backend) who is experienced and brings deep expertise in backend development. You will join a small, talented team and lead efforts to architect scalable solutions, drive technical excellence, and support our vision for a future where Grass plays a critical role in shaping the internet's evolution.

The company

Gottex Brokers is the leading interdealer broker in interest rate derivative products across continental Europe. Based in Lausanne, Switzerland, we provide a service of excellence to our customers worldwide with particular acclaim for our Swiss Franc and Scandinavian interest rate derivatives and fixed income products.

With over 30 years of industry experience, we offer a bespoke service while maintaining the highest levels of professionalism and integrity. We have a dynamic, multinational team of experienced and highly qualified employees providing local expertise to our international client base.

As the global reference for Swiss Franc interest rate derivatives, and a dominant force in the Scandinavian markets, we offer unrivalled, customized solutions to our clients, while maintaining the highest levels of professionalism and integrity.

Our experienced and internationally diverse team serves to reinforce the strength of our brand, which has been a hallmark of the OTC derivatives business since its foundation in 1986.


Corporate culture


Excellence in execution.

We bring an outstanding and personalised service with a long-lasting commitment to our clients through a deep-rooted family culture, imbued with respect and shared values.


Note more:

Attention – Restricted Target Companies

The client has identified specific companies from which active poaching is strictly forbidden. Only passive approaches are allowed for the following institutions: Tradition, Conticap, and Starcb / banks.

Candidate Profile – Key Requirements

Experience within the Inter-Dealer Broker sector is strongly desired. Candidates must come from competitors, and active headhunting is required except for the restricted companies listed above. Banker profiles are not acceptable.

Experience in the following companies would be considered a strong advantage: Martin Brokers, Bondpartners, Bridport, Valcourt, Kepler Cheuvreux, Tullett Prebon, BGC, GFI Group, Pareto Securities AG, Aurel, BGC Freedom International Brokerage, Stifel Schweiz AG, and Louis Capital.

Flexibility and Compensation

Compensation is open to negotiation depending on the candidate’s experience and existing client portfolio. The candidate must already manage an established client book. There is flexibility on remuneration based on the seniority of the candidate and the size and quality of their client portfolio.

Administrative Constraints

The client does not accept candidates who require administrative procedures for a work permit or residence permit. Only candidates already authorized to work in the relevant location will be considered.

Position – Projects, Issues and Priorities

The ideal candidate will have a strong knowledge of financial markets, with a specific focus on FX Forward CHF. Long-standing and existing relationships with interbank traders are mandatory, with the ability to leverage these relationships immediately.

There is no possibility of promotion associated with this position.

Organization and Reporting Line

The team consists of six people. This role does not include any management responsibilities. The position reports directly to the Head of IRS Desk.

title: Senior Account Executive, Financial Institutions & Banks -Conduit

jobsdetail.description:
Conduit is seeking a Director of Banking Sales to drive commercial growth by selling our cross-border payments and infrastructure solutions to banks, including Tier 1 institutions to regional and community banks. This is a senior, high-impact role for someone who understands how banks operate, has a strong existing network across the U.S. banking ecosystem, and can independently generate new opportunities and relationships.


You’ll lead the go-to-market strategy for the banking segment - creating compelling sales materials, building tailored solution proposals, and working hand-in-hand with product, compliance, and engineering teams to design and implement bespoke cross-border payment flows. This role combines strategic sales leadership with technical and operational fluency, ideal for someone who can sell to banks and speak their language.


We’re looking for a builder: someone entrepreneurial, execution-oriented, and equally comfortable in a boardroom with banking c-suite as in the weeds with a payments operations team.


Key Responsibilities


Own the banking sales pipeline: Build and manage a robust pipeline of bank relationships across Tier 1, regional, and community banks. Drive the full sales lifecycle from outreach to closing.

Develop strategic relationships: Leverage your existing network to establish and expand partnerships that enable Conduit’s global cross-border payment flows.

Design and present solutions: Create compelling commercial proposals and payment flow structures aligned with each bank’s technical, compliance, and operational frameworks.

Lead sales enablement: Develop and refine sales collateral, pitch decks, and product positioning to effectively communicate Conduit’s value to financial institutions.

Collaborate cross-functionally: Work closely with compliance, legal, product, and engineering to ensure proposed solutions align with regulatory and operational requirements.

Guide onboarding and implementation: Partner with internal teams to ensure smooth bank integration, onboarding, and go-live processes, with a focus on client satisfaction and retention.

Drive outbound business development: Proactively identify and pursue new banking relationships — you’ll be expected to build and manage your own outbound pipeline.

Represent Conduit externally: Attend industry events, conferences, and client meetings to enhance Conduit’s visibility within the U.S. and global banking and fintech ecosystem.
jobsdetail.requirement:
Qualifications


Experience: 8+ years in enterprise sales or partnership roles focused on financial institutions — preferably in payments, fintech infrastructure, or B2B SaaS.

Banking network: Strong relationships across U.S. banks (Tier 1, regional, and community), with a proven ability to originate and close high-value partnerships.

Technical fluency: Solid understanding of how banks operate — core banking systems, payment rails (ACH, Fedwire, SWIFT, RTP), compliance, and treasury operations.

Sales leadership: Proven success designing sales strategies, structuring complex deals, and working cross-functionally to deliver client-ready solutions.

Entrepreneurial mindset: A self-starter who thrives in ambiguity, builds from scratch, and moves quickly from idea to execution.

Communication: Exceptional written and verbal communication skills, able to engage credibly with C-suite executives and technical stakeholders alike.

Bonus: Experience in cross-border payments, correspondent banking, or stablecoin-based payment infrastructure.

title: Founding Engineer (Full Stack) – AI Platform for Litigation w/ 0.25%–1.0% Equity- Crimson
location: London, UK (3+ days/week in-office near Bank)

jobsdetail.description:
Job Overview

As a Founding Engineer (Full Stack) for our AI Platform for Litigation, you'll play a crucial role in designing and implementing a cutting-edge system tailored for high-stakes legal environments. You'll work closely with legal professionals to integrate AI-driven solutions that enhance litigation processes. Comprehensive Stack Contribution: Lead initiatives across cloud infrastructure, Python backend, and Next.js frontend, while managing large datasets with PostgreSQL and Azure-hosted LLMs. ...


Responsibilities

This is a founding full-stack engineering role. You’ll join Crimson as one of the first employees and help build the AI platform that powers litigation at leading UK and US law firms.


What you’ll do:

• Contribute across the entire stack, from cloud infrastructure to prompting to UX – including Python backend, Next.js + TypeScript frontend, PostgreSQL, and Azure-hosted LLM workloads with CI/CD pipelines (GitHub Actions, IaC such as Bicep).

• Collaborate closely with litigators and knowledge workers to understand how lawyers work, what they need, and how Crimson can fit into their workflows.

• Architect and scale document ingestion and processing pipelines to power fast, accurate search, summarisation and data extraction over large volumes of legal documents.

• Design and implement intelligent, multi-step agent workflows that can autonomously handle complex legal tasks – from surfacing key testimony to generating fact chronologies and spotting inconsistencies in filings.

• Design and bring to life intuitive, AI-native user experiences tailored to litigation workflows, in partnership with the founders and early customers.

• Improve system performance, stability and observability as we scale to more cases, larger datasets and additional firms.

• Help shape our engineering culture and team – from best practices and code standards to interviewing, hiring and mentoring future engineers.
jobsdetail.requirement:
Qualifications

3+ years of experience building and shipping production web applications (full-stack), ideally in startups or small, high-ownership teams.

Strong backend experience with Python (or similar), PostgreSQL and cloud infrastructure (Azure preferred; AWS or GCP also relevant).

Frontend experience with modern TypeScript frameworks (Next.js / React) and an eye for building clean, intuitive product experiences.

Experience designing and operating data pipelines or distributed systems that handle large volumes of text / documents.

Comfort working with LLMs and applied AI – prompting, integrating model APIs, and thinking carefully about correctness, reliability and UX for AI-powered features.

Excellent product judgement, communication skills and attention to detail – you’re comfortable talking to demanding users and turning their feedback into shipped features.

High agency and bias for shipping; you like owning problems end-to-end and seeing your work used in real cases.

Bonus: interest or background in legal tech, disputes, or working with professional services users.


Ideal Candidate

Ideal Candidate Profile


Driven and self-directed – you take ownership of hard problems, operate with high agency, and care deeply about getting to a great outcome for demanding users.

Product-obsessed full-stack builder – you’re as comfortable in Python/Postgres as you are in TypeScript/React, and you enjoy sweating the UX details that make AI feel reliable and usable.

User-centric communicator – you like talking to lawyers, partners and associates, translating messy workflows into clean technical systems, and iterating quickly based on feedback.

Excited by AI in high-stakes domains – you want to push the limits of LLMs in production while caring deeply about accuracy, security and reliability for billion-pound disputes.

Bonus: prior experience in legal tech, fintech, or other professional-services products where users are smart, time-poor, and extremely quality-sensitive.

About company:

About the Company

Company Overview

Company Size: 5-10

Industry: AI-powered legaltech for litigation; software development

Specialties: Litigation, arbitration, dispute resolution, legal technology, and artificial intelligence

Founded: 2024

Headquarters: London, United Kingdom

Total Funding: US$500k+ pre-seed (Y Combinator and other investors; YC X25 batch)

https://www.crimson.law/


Our Mission

Crimson is an AI productivity platform built for litigators. Litigation teams use Crimson to analyze case files, answer case-specific questions and draft documents tailored to each dispute. By automating manual workflows across the life cycle of a case, Crimson helps lawyers find key evidence faster, streamline case management and save 10+ hours per week on common litigation tasks.


Company Culture


Crimson is a small, high-ownership team of three co-founders with deep technical and disputes expertise. They’re building a category-defining AI platform for high-stakes litigation, working with major UK and US law firms that trust them with highly sensitive case files. The culture is product-driven and security-obsessed: engineers talk directly to users, own features end-to-end, and care deeply about accuracy, reliability and speed. Backed by Y Combinator and other top investors, the team is focused on shipping quickly, learning from demanding users and setting a new standard for legal AI.


What you can expect

Day to Day

You’ll spend most of your time designing, building, and operating the full-stack systems that power Crimson’s AI platform for high-stakes litigation. Your work ships quickly to elite UK and US law firms and is used on live cases within days.


In practice, that looks like:


Contributing across the stack – from Python backend and document pipelines to Next.js + TypeScript frontend, PostgreSQL, and Azure-hosted LLM workloads (with IaC + CI/CD).

Collaborating directly with litigators and knowledge workers to understand how they run disputes today, where the bottlenecks are, and how Crimson can slot into their workflows.

Designing and scaling ingestion, search and summarisation pipelines over large volumes of pleadings, transcripts, evidence, and orders.

Building intelligent, multi-step agent workflows that automate complex legal tasks, while keeping humans in the loop for high-stakes decisions.

Iterating rapidly based on user feedback – instrumenting features, debugging real cases, and improving reliability and performance as more firms and matters come onto the platform.

Team

You’ll join a small, senior founding team that blends deep AI expertise with top-tier litigation experience.


Crimson was founded by Amine Amor (ML/AI specialist), David Strömbäck (AI engineer), and Mark Feldner (former BigLaw litigator).

You’ll work day-to-day with the technical founders on architecture, roadmap, and implementation, and partner closely with Mark to understand how litigators actually use the product in high-stakes cases.

The team is small, high-ownership, and product-driven – everyone ships code, talks to users, and has a strong voice in what gets built next.

Growth

This is a founding-level role with a lot of surface area and real upside:


Own major parts of Crimson’s architecture and product from day one and shape how the core litigation platform evolves over the coming years.

Help define engineering culture from scratch – everything from code standards and observability to how we run interviews and onboard future engineers.

Build deep relationships with elite UK and US law firms and see your work used on complex, cross-border disputes that matter.

Grow into a tech lead / founding-team engineering leader as the company scales – with meaningful equity in a YC-backed, category-defining legal AI company.


Interview Process

Intro Call (30 min) – Introductory conversation with one of the founders to learn more about your background, walk through Crimson’s product and users, and assess mutual fit.

Technical Deep Dive (60–75 min) – Live discussion with a founder covering your experience building and shipping full-stack products, system-design style questions, and how you’ve handled complex, real-world requirements.

Practical Exercise / Case Study (60–90 min) – Short take-home or live exercise focused on how you’d design and implement a Crimson-style feature (for example, a document ingestion + search workflow or an AI-assisted litigation task), followed by a review with the team.

Founder Round (60–90 min) – Conversations with the founding team to go deeper on collaboration style, product thinking, and mission alignment, plus any remaining technical topics.

Track record of owning features end-to-end in a small, high-ownership or startup environment, including talking directly to users.

Based in (or willing to relocate to) London, with existing right to work in the UK and ability to be in the office at least 3 days per week.

jobsdetail.description:
Job Overview

Responsibilities: Owning Core AI and Backend Systems: Design and manage ML systems for ingesting and analyzing large evidence volumes. Data Retrieval Architecture: Create reliable retrieval/RAG pipelines for quick investigator access to structured and unstructured data. Model Prototyping: Experiment with LLMs and other novel tools, developing robust production systems for law enforcement. User Collaboration: Implement ML features using feedback from detectives and prosecutors, enhancing...

Responsibilities

This is a founding AI / backend engineering role. You’ll design and ship the ML systems that power Closure’s “digital analyst” for law enforcement—working closely with the founders, Forward-Deployed Engineers, and investigators in the field.


What you will do:


Own core AI and backend systems that ingest, process, and search across large volumes of evidence (calls, reports, documents, transcripts, and more).

Design and implement retrieval / RAG pipelines for unstructured and structured data, making it fast and reliable for investigators to find what they need.

Prototype with new models and tools (LLMs, embeddings, vector databases, observability stack), then harden the best ideas into production systems agencies can trust.

Collaborate closely with Forward-Deployed Engineers and users to turn real-world feedback from detectives and prosecutors into concrete ML features and ranking improvements.

Contribute across the stack when needed (APIs, internal tools, evaluation dashboards) to keep the overall AI surface area robust, monitored, and maintainable.
jobsdetail.requirement:
Qualifications

3+ years of professional software engineering experience with strong backend fundamentals (distributed systems, APIs, data modeling) in a modern stack (e.g., Python + TypeScript/React or similar).

Hands-on experience building and shipping ML/AI systems used by real users, ideally involving LLMs or other deep-learning models (not just research or PoCs).

Experience with retrieval / RAG or similar architectures over unstructured text or multi-modal data (documents, transcripts, logs), including designing data pipelines and evaluation approaches.

Comfortable working end-to-end: from understanding investigator workflows and problem framing, to designing experiments, to deploying and monitoring models in production.

Strong communication and collaboration skills; able to work directly with founders, Forward-Deployed Engineers, and non-technical stakeholders in a small, fast-moving, mission-driven team.


Ideal Candidate

Ideal Candidate Profile


Field-Driven Engineer – Strong full-stack engineer (Python + modern frontend) who enjoys leaving the office, sitting with users, and seeing how software actually gets used in the wild.

Customer-Obsessed Problem Solver – Comfortable building trust with detectives and agency leadership, asking good questions, and turning messy requirements into clear product and technical decisions.

High-Ownership Operator – Thrives in tiny, fast-moving teams, takes full responsibility for deployments and outcomes, and is happy to do whatever the situation requires (from debugging to running training sessions).

Mission-Motivated – Energized by improving public safety and the criminal-justice system, and comfortable working with sensitive, sometimes difficult case material.

Startup-Ready – Has prior experience in early-stage or talent-dense environments and is excited by ambiguity, rapid iteration, and having a big say in how the product and company evolve.

Company Overview

Company Size: 5-10 employees

Industry:Govtech, public safety, law-enforcement technology, and AI / machine learning

Specialties: Digital evidence analysis, AI-powered transcription and translation, semantic search across case files, investigations and case management, criminal justice technology

Founded: 2024

Headquarters: New York, New York

https://www.closure-intel.com/


Our Mission

Closure helps law enforcement, prosecutors, attorneys, and investigators quickly find the truth in overwhelming volumes of digital evidence. Their “digital analyst” platform securely transcribes, translates, searches, organizes, and analyzes case files—such as jail communications, search-warrant returns, and scanned documents—across many languages. By turning evidence overload into clear, searchable insight, Closure lets agencies focus on solving cases and delivering accurate, fair justice instead of manually combing through data.

Company Culture

Closure is a small, seed-stage, YC-backed team built around mission-driven, high-ownership work in public safety. The founders are experienced engineers from Palantir and defense backgrounds who care deeply about helping government work better. The culture emphasizes thoughtful, field-driven product development with investigators and prosecutors, rapid iteration on real-world problems, and a high bar for reliability and trust in how AI is applied. Engineers are expected to be hands-on with users, comfortable with ambiguity, and motivated by impact rather than big-company structure.


What you can expect

Day to Day

You’ll spend your days designing, building, and operating the AI and backend systems that power Closure’s “digital analyst” for law enforcement.

In practice, that looks like:

• Owning core data and ML pipelines that ingest, process, and search across large volumes of evidence (calls, reports, transcripts, documents, etc.).

• Designing and implementing retrieval / RAG architectures over unstructured and structured data so investigators can reliably find what matters.

• Prototyping with new models and tools (LLMs, embeddings, vector databases, observability) and turning the best ideas into robust, production-ready systems.

• Partnering closely with Forward-Deployed Engineers and founders to turn field feedback from detectives and prosecutors into concrete ML features and ranking improvements.

• Contributing across the stack when needed — from APIs and job orchestration to evaluation dashboards and monitoring — to keep the entire AI surface area healthy.

Team

You’ll join a tiny, senior founding team of ex-Palantir and defense engineers who are obsessed with solving meaningful problems in public safety.

• You’ll report directly to Gilad Levy (Co-founder & CTO) and work closely with Aaron Zelinger (Co-founder) on architecture, roadmap, and product direction.

• You’ll collaborate tightly with Forward-Deployed Engineers who spend time in the field with investigators and bring back high-signal problems and feedback.

• The team is lean, highly technical, and mission-driven — everyone ships code, talks to users, and has a strong voice in what gets built next.

Growth

This is a founding-level AI role with a lot of surface area:

• Own major pieces of Closure’s AI and backend architecture from day one and shape how the digital analyst evolves over time.

• Define best practices for evaluating, monitoring, and hardening LLM / retrieval systems in high-stakes law-enforcement settings.

• Influence product strategy by connecting what’s technically possible with what investigators actually need in the field.

• Help hire and mentor future ML/AI engineers as the company grows, building out a small but world-class applied AI team.

• Build a rare track record of shipping real-world, high-impact AI systems at a YC-backed, public-safety-focused startup.


Initial Call (30 min)

– Introductory conversation with a founder to learn more about your background, walk through the role, and assess mutual fit.

Technical Deep Dive (60–75 min) – Live discussion with an engineer covering your experience building and shipping products, system-design style questions, and how you’ve handled messy real-world requirements.

Practical Exercise / Case Study (60–90 min) – Short take-home or live exercise focused on how you’d approach a forward-deployed problem (e.g., understanding a customer workflow and translating it into product/technical changes).

Final Round (60–90 min) – Conversations with founders and team members to go deeper on collaboration style, working with customers, mission alignment, and any remaining technical topics.

Technical Qualifications: 5+ years of ML (PyTorch, TensorFlow) and 2+ years with LLMs. Proven track record in deploying production AI systems.

Responsibilities

This is a founding Forward-Deployed Engineer role. You sit at the intersection of product, engineering and field work. You will embed directly with detectives, analysts and prosecutors, learn their workflows in detail, and use that context to deploy Closure’s platform and shape the roadmap.


This is a full-time role for an engineer who wants to own real problems end to end, work closely with users, and see their work show up in live investigations.

  1. Spend significant time on-site with law-enforcement customers to understand how they investigate cases and where evidence review slows them down.

  5. Help design and refine processes for pilots, rollouts and training so that new departments can adopt Closure smoothly.
  6. Act as a trusted technical partner for investigators and leadership teams, helping them understand what is possible with the product.

Our Mission

Closure helps law enforcement, prosecutors, attorneys, and investigators quickly find the truth in overwhelming volumes of digital evidence. Their “digital analyst” platform securely transcribes, translates, searches, organizes, and analyzes case files—such as jail communications, search-warrant returns, and scanned documents—across many languages. By turning evidence overload into clear, searchable insight, Closure lets agencies focus on solving cases and delivering accurate, fair justice instead of manually combing through data.

Company Culture

Closure is a small, seed-stage, YC-backed team built around mission-driven, high-ownership work in public safety. The founders are experienced engineers from Palantir and defense backgrounds who care deeply about helping government work better. The culture emphasizes thoughtful, field-driven product development with investigators and prosecutors, rapid iteration on real-world problems, and a high bar for reliability and trust in how AI is applied. Engineers are expected to be hands-on with users, comfortable with ambiguity, and motivated by impact rather than big-company structure.


What you can expect

Day to Day

You’ll spend most of your time in the field with users and turning their needs into product. In practice, that looks like:

  1. Embedding on-site with detectives, analysts, and prosecutors to watch how they investigate cases, understand their workflows, and uncover pain points.
  2. Deploying and configuring Closure’s platform for new departments, loading data, troubleshooting issues, and making sure investigators can rely on the system in day-to-day work.
  3. Translating what you see in the field into clear product ideas and engineering tasks, then building and shipping full-stack features using Python, React, TypeScript, and AI/ML APIs.
  4. Owning pilots end-to-end: planning deployment, running training sessions, gathering feedback, and iterating quickly with the founding team.
  5. Traveling regularly (25–75% of your time) to customer sites across the U.S. while staying tightly looped in with the core team in New York.


Team

You’ll join a tiny, senior founding team of ex-Palantir and ex-IDF engineers who are obsessed with solving meaningful problems in public safety.

  1. You’ll report directly to Aaron Zelinger (Co-founder) and work closely with the other founders on both product and customer work.
  2. The team is lean, highly technical, and mission-driven — everyone ships code, talks to users, and contributes to product direction.
  3. As one of the first Forward-Deployed Engineers, you’ll shape how the FDE function works at Closure, from playbooks for deployments to how feedback flows back into the roadmap.

Interview Process

  1. Initial Call (30 min) – Introductory conversation with a founder to learn more about your background, walk through the role, and assess mutual fit.

  3. Practical Exercise / Case Study (60–90 min) – Short take-home or live exercise focused on how you’d approach a forward-deployed problem (e.g., understanding a customer workflow and translating it into product/technical changes).
  4. Final Round (60–90 min) – Conversations with founders and team members to go deeper on collaboration style, working with customers, mission alignment, and any remaining technical topics.


Growth

This is a founding-level role with a lot of surface area:

  1. Own critical customer deployments and relationships from day one, becoming the go-to technical partner for some of Closure’s key law-enforcement agencies.
  2. Directly influence product direction by bringing field context to every roadmap discussion and helping decide what gets built next.
  3. Help define and later scale the Forward-Deployed Engineering function — including processes, tooling, and eventually mentoring or leading additional FDEs as the team grows.
  4. Build a rare track record of high-impact, user-embedded engineering work at a YC-backed, seed-stage startup tackling complex, real-world problems.

You’ll be stepping into a high-impact design leadership role where craft meets scale. Arist is looking for a designer who’s not only exceptional at creating beautiful, intuitive interfaces but who’s also led design for products that have grown to meaningful traction — real customers, real revenue, and measurable outcomes. The ideal candidate has shaped SaaS experiences at scale (7–8 figures in ARR), understands the nuances of building with AI, and thrives in fast-moving startup environments where design decisions directly influence company growth.

  • 8+ years of experience in UX/UI or Product Design, ideally within high-growth SaaS environments.
  • Proven track record leading product design for platforms generating 7–8 figures in ARR, demonstrating deep ownership from concept to launch.
  • Strong proficiency with Figma and modern design systems.
  • Experience designing AI-powered products or interfaces that leverage machine learning, automation, or personalization.

  • Data-driven mindset with sharp product intuition and a bias toward simplicity and clarity in design.

Ideal Candidate

What makes you a strong fit

  • Owns end-to-end product experience at scale — led design for SaaS products that reached 7–8 figures ARR, translating complex workflows (admin + learner) into intuitive flows and measurable outcomes (usability, NPS, self-serve rates).
  • AI & data-literate — comfortable designing AI-powered experiences (automation, personalization, assistants, RAG-like flows) and using product analytics/UAT to prioritize work.
  • Design systems + speed — builds/maintains robust design systems in Figma, ships quickly with engineers, and raises quality through clear specs, reviews, and UAT.
  • Startup-ready leadership — collaborates tightly with founders/Eng/Product/CS, can interview customers, run discovery, and make crisp, business-aware trade-offs. YC or similar venture-backed experience is a plus.

Benchmark profile (from founders)

  • Senior/Staff Product Designer (or Design Lead) from a YC or high-growth SaaS company (e.g., Retool, Vanta, Scale AI, Linear, Segment) who has: owned a core surface, shipped roadmap with clear metrics, and stood up/leveled a design system.

Signals we’re likely to pass

  • Portfolio is mostly visual polish with little evidence of shipped, user-validated impact (no metrics, no before/after).
  • No enterprise/ops workflow depth (only marketing sites or B2C surface work).
  • Can’t work US hours or needs future visa sponsorship.
  • Limited collaboration stories with Eng/PM or weak rationale behind decisions.

Why this is exciting

  • Direct line to founders, shaping a mission product used by global enterprises.
  • Chance to define the admin + learner experience and the design system that powers it.
  • Your work moves business metrics (self-serve, satisfaction, adoption) and learning outcomes—fast feedback loops, fast shipping.

Inspiration portfolios (do not contact — for calibration only)

These public portfolios show narrative quality, shipped impact, and senior craft. Useful for bar-setting when reviewing candidates:

  1. https://www.linkedin.com/in/sethweisfeld/— Senior Staff Product Designer (Meta); strong storytelling across complex surfaces and new mediums (GenAI, video).
  2. https://www.benprudden.co.uk/ — Senior Product Designer (healthtech + fintech); clear outcomes and large-scale patient experiences.
  3. https://malugin.me/resume — Senior Product Designer (Resume.io/Career.io); design systems + data-driven improvements at significant consumer scale.

Note: These are public examples to calibrate the bar/style of case studies (impact, metrics, rationale). I can also assemble a shortlist focused on US-based, SaaS, YC-adjacent designers if you want direct outreach targets (e.g., Senior/Staff from Retool, Vanta, Ramp, Mercury, Notion, Linear).

title: Lifecycle / Retention Manager (Braze) - Buy Moonshots, Inc.
location: Open to remote US/Canada but MUST be available for in person sprints in NYC - Preference for full time in NYC

jobsdetail.description:
Description

Your Moonshot


Moonshot is the “Robinhood for DeFi”—an iOS and Android app that lets anyone trade cryptocurrencies on-chain through a user-friendly interface. Since our launch in July 2024, we’ve attracted 2+ million users. At Moonshot, our mission is straightforward yet ambitious: to onboard the world to decentralised finance. We believe in the power of crypto and blockchain technology to build a future where finance is open and accessible to everyone.


We’re a lean team of passionate self-starters who relish the responsibility that comes with autonomy. Everyone on our team is both a decision-maker and an implementer—united by a sense of urgency and driven by the conviction that our work can transform global finance. If you thrive on curiosity, welcome tough challenges, and aspire to leave a meaningful mark on the world, Moonshot is the place for you. You’ll run toward the hardest problems alongside high-caliber teammates who consistently push each other to excel and who share your dedication to shaping the future of decentralised finance.


The Opportunity


As a Lifecycle / Retention Manager (Braze) at Moonshot, you’ll own Braze end-to-end: email, push, in-app messaging, segmentation, journeys, and reactivation. Experience in sports betting, crypto, consumer apps, or trading environments is a strong plus.


Responsibilities:


Own all lifecycle messaging in Braze (email, push, in-app, inbox)

Build and maintain automated onboarding, retention, and win-back flows

Create dynamic segmentation and triggered journeys

Set up and monitor promotions and targeted incentives

Run A/B tests and weekly lifecycle experiments

Track activation → retention → reactivation KPIs and report learnings

5+ years in Lifecycle / CRM / Retention for a consumer app

Comfort using data to inform decisions

Understanding of trader psychology, incentives, and habit loops

Remote US

title: Founding Engineer -Buy Moonshots, Inc.
location: US Remote (Must be able to travel to NYC 2 weeks at a time, every 1-2 months for in person collaboration)

jobsdetail.description:
Tip

Candidates from high growth consumer Apps (Preferably mobile)

They recently provided us a profile of someone who would be an excellent addition for them. The candidate had a background working at impressive reputable companies i.e BitPay & then transitioned into Blockchain companies who are mobile app focussed i.e Coinbase / Phantom. They also have an extensive Software Engineering background with 5+ years experience, before transitioning into a Product Engineer.

Description

Your Moonshot

Moonshot is the “Robinhood for DeFi”—an iOS and Android app that lets anyone trade cryptocurrencies on-chain through a user-friendly interface. Since our launch in July 2024, we’ve attracted 1.5+ million users.

At Moonshot, our mission is straightforward yet ambitious: to onboard the world to decentralized finance. We believe in the power of crypto and blockchain technology to build a future where finance is open and accessible to everyone.

We’re a lean team of passionate self-starters who relish the responsibility that comes with autonomy. Everyone on our team is both a decision-maker and an implementer—united by a sense of urgency and driven by the conviction that our work can transform global finance.

If you thrive on curiosity, welcome tough challenges, and aspire to leave a meaningful mark on the world, Moonshot is the place for you. You’ll run toward the hardest problems alongside high-caliber teammates who consistently push each other to excel and who share your dedication to shaping the future of decentralised finance.

The Opportunity

As our Founding Engineer, you’ll assume a hands-on role in designing, building, and maintaining the production features that power our rapidly growing DeFi trading platform. You’ll be responsible for optimising developer velocity, ensuring platform reliability, and fostering a security-first culture.

If you’re excited by the challenge of building beautiful products for millions of users in a cutting-edge Web3 ecosystem—we want to hear from you.

What You’ll Be Doing

Product Ownership

Design, build, and ship delightful, high-impact features end-to-end across mobile and backend—shaping the core user experience of the Moonshot app.

Rapid Iteration

Collaborate closely with design, product, and growth teams to prototype, test, and launch new ideas quickly—shipping often and learning fast.

User-Centric Development

Dive deep into user feedback and product analytics to inform decisions, prioritize enhancements, and continuously improve usability and performance.

Cross-Platform Engineering

Own full-stack features from Flutter frontends to Go backends, with an emphasis on clean architecture and high performance across iOS and Android.

Quality & Reliability

Uphold engineering best practices, write high-quality code with strong test coverage, and participate in code reviews to maintain velocity without sacrificing stability.

Team Collaboration

Partner with engineers, designers, and founders to make product decisions, set milestones, and shape Moonshot’s technical direction.

title: Senior Frontend Engineer- Reya Labs Limited

jobsdetail.description:
Comment (20.01.2026)

Reya would still like to see profiles for this role - they want to see profiles that have solid trading and Web3 experience. This is a hard requirement for them

----------

As a Senior Frontend Engineer, you will be driving the development of a performant, responsive, real-time frontend application that powers the Reya DeX and Network. In this role, you will work closely with the team to create a seamless user experience that meets the demands of a fast-paced and data-intensive environment.


We're a dynamic, forward-thinking, fully remote team with an ambitious roadmap and a relentless drive to make things happen.


The ideal candidate is someone who loves DeFi, web3, engineering, security, rigor and speed. If you're a highly skilled Senior Frontend engineer with over eight years of experience and a commitment to code quality and state-of-the-art software testing practices, we want to hear from you.


Responsibilities


Real-Time Interface Development: Design and implement dynamic, interactive user interfaces that can handle real-time data feeds, such as live order book updates, price updates, trade executions. Your work will directly impact the user experience for traders.


Collaboration & Cross-Functional Leadership: Partner with the product team and UX/UI designer to translate complex requirements into engaging, intuitive interfaces. Work side-by-side with backend engineers to ensure smooth data flow and API integration.


Performance Optimization: Optimize application performance through efficient code, advanced rendering techniques, and smart resource management. You will focus on minimizing load times and ensuring smooth, lag-free interactions even under heavy data loads.


Responsive Design: Build responsive and accessible frontends that perform consistently across different devices and browsers, ensuring a high-quality user experience on desktops, tablets, and mobile devices.


Modern Frontend Architecture: Utilize modern frontend frameworks (such as React) and state management libraries to architect scalable and maintainable codebases. Embrace best practices in component design, testing, and documentation.

6+ years in frontend development with a solid background in building high-performance, real-time web applications

Deep expertise in JavaScript & TypeScript, modern web frameworks, and advanced CSS techniques.

Deep knowledge of the architecture of performant client-side React applications

Comfortable working in a dynamic environment where priorities may shift rapidly. Your proactive approach and strong communication skills help drive project success.

You have a strong commitment to writing clean, maintainable code and are well-versed in testing methodologies including unit, integration, and end-to-end testing.

Demonstrated experience with web sockets or other real-time data protocol.

Prior experience working closely with product, design, and backend teams in a remote or distributed setting


Preferred Qualifications

Proven experience (8+ years) in frontend development, particularly within fintech, trading, or real-time data applications.

Knowledge of server-side rendering (e.g., Next.js) and progressive web app (PWA) development is a plus

Familiarity with cloud platforms and continuous integration/continuous deployment (CI/CD) pipelines to streamline development and delivery processes

Bachelor's or Master's degree in Computer Science or a related field or equivalent experience

Familiarity with the web3 frontend stack (ethers/viem, client-side private key management, etc.)

jobsdetail.description:
Comment 20.01.2026

Reya would still like to see profiles for this role - they want to see profiles that have solid trading and Web3 experience. This is a hard requirement for them


Recruiting Tips

They're looking for someone who can quickly get up to speed, take ownership, and execute effectively with minimal oversight.

Ideally, this person has deep experience in crypto - particularly with trading platforms and understands the nuances of decentralized exchanges and liquidity systems.

Currently, they operate perpetual markets on the Reya Network to provide liquidity. The next phase of their journey is to build a roll-up–based infrastructure on Ethereum, designed specifically for internet scale capital markets. Their goal is to address many of the structural inefficiencies of traditional finance by leveraging crypto.

This means building a system with sub-millisecond block times, near-zero trading fees, and seamless integration with Ethereum - bringing real-world assets and institutional-grade capital markets fully on-chain.


Senior DevOps Engineer – Reya Labs

As a Senior DevOps Engineer, you will play a pivotal role in building and maintaining the infrastructure that powers the Reya DeX and Network. Your mission is to ensure our systems are secure, scalable, and highly available — enabling the team to deliver quickly without sacrificing reliability.

We're a dynamic, forward-thinking, fully remote team with an ambitious roadmap and a relentles drive to make things happen.

The ideal candidate is someone who loves automation, reliability engineering, infrastructure design, security, and performance. If you're a highly skilled DevOps engineer with over eight years of experience in infrastructure and CI/CD systems — and a commitment to security, observability, and operational excellence — we want to hear from you.


Responsibilities

Infrastructure Design & Automation:

Design, implement, and maintain cloud-native infrastructure using Infrastructure as Code (IaC). Build scalable, reproducible environments that support high-throughput, low-latency trading systems.

CI/CD & Developer Experience:

Own and improve the CI/CD pipelines for backend, frontend, and smart contract codebases. Ensure fast, reliable builds and deployments with strong observability and rollback mechanism.

Monitoring, Observability & Reliability:

Define and implement monitoring, logging, and alerting strategies to achieve maximum system reliability and early incident detection. Drive SLO/SLI/SLAs and participate in on-call rotations.

Scalability & Performance:

Optimize infrastructure for latency-sensitive workloads. Tune autoscaling policies, network configurations, and resource utilization to ensure consistent performance during peak loads.

Security & Compliance:

Implement robust security practices across infrastructure, CI/CD, and runtime environments. Protect systems from evolving threats through network segmentation, secret management, and runtime hardening.

Incident Response & Disaster Recovery:

Establish and maintain playbooks, failover mechanisms, and disaster recovery plans. Lead post-incident reviews and proactively address root causes.

Cost Management:

Continuously monitor and optimize cloud costs while ensuring performance and redundancy requirements are met.

Technological Evaluation:

Stay current on infrastructure tooling, orchestration frameworks, and security best practices. Evaluate new technologies for potential adoption to improve performance and resilience.

Documentation & Knowledge Sharing:

Maintain clear, detailed documentation for infrastructure, processes, and runbooks. Mentor engineers and help foster a culture of reliability and automation across the team.

Advanced knowledge of monitoring, logging, and alerting tools (Prometheus, Grafana, ELK/EFK, OpenTelemetry)

Proven ability to manage and tune production infrastructure supporting latency-sensitive workloads

Strong scripting skills (Python, Bash, Go, or similar)

Excellent communication and collaboration skills with a focus on reliability and continuous improvement


Preferred Qualifications

Experience supporting low-latency trading systems or other real-time financial applications

Updated the JD and hiring criteria. This role is now a major focus for Predicate, we have just placed 2 people there.

They would be interested in anyone from an L1/ L2 that understands blockchains, blockchain providers or RPC's


Unlock the next era of public blockchain networks

About us

We’re building Predicate, a platform setting the standard for how regulated blockchain applications enforce transaction rules at the smart contract layer. Predicate empowers teams to create modular, programmable policies—such as AML/CFT checks or offchain collateral requirements—that are verified before a transaction executes onchain. Our system enforces these policies with cryptographic guarantees, providing robust, scalable risk management tailored to real-world use cases.

The role

As Protocol Lead, you’ll own the design, development, and maintenance of Predicate’s onchain suite. You’ll build tooling to support integrations, contribute to core infrastructure, and research emerging technologies to keep us at the forefront of the industry. You’ll also serve as the team’s blockchain subject matter expert, supporting teammates across the stack on all things related to blockchain interactions.

Responsibilities

  1. Own the architecture, development, and maintenance of Predicate’s core smart contracts
  2. Support customers integrating Predicate at the smart contract level
  3. Deeply understand and add Predicate support for non-EVM chains
  4. Iterate on the Predicate SDK and relevant documentation to supply a strong integration experience
  5. Collaborate with the rest of the engineering team in Go and TypeScript on blockchain facing technology

jobsdetail.description:
Important: Please read the following before referring candidates

They have a high bar on the role, good education and well-known companies that people have worked at is usually a good signal for them. If you are sending candidates from outside of North America please make sure they have good tenures at reputable companies.


Description

About Rain

At Rain, we’re rebuilding the global financial pipes money flows through. Our infrastructure makes stablecoins usable in the real world by powering credit card transactions, cross-border payments, B2B purchases, remittances, and more. We partner with fintechs, neobanks, and institutions to help them launch solutions that are global, inclusive, and efficient. If you’re curious, bold, and excited to help shape a borderless financial system, we’d love to talk.

Our Ethos

Operating at the epicenter of stablecoin innovation means moving fast and thinking globally. Our team reflects the diverse, international audiences we serve. We hire people who stay agile as the tide ebbs and flows, fix what’s broken without waiting, chase trends before they peak, and remember to have fun through it all.


About the Role


We’re looking for Rain’s first dedicated Data Engineer—a hands-on builder who will architect the ingestion, pipelines, and infrastructure that power our data ecosystem. As Rain scales to millions of end users across payments, card programs, and blockchain rails, your systems will ensure every team has access to timely, accurate, and trustworthy data.


Reporting to the CTO and partnering closely with Analytics, Operations, Product, Compliance, and BD, you will own Rain’s data pipelines end-to-end: from ingesting raw transaction and blockchain data, to orchestrating transformations, to ensuring quality, observability, and reliability. This role is foundational—you’ll be shaping the data backbone that underpins analytics, customer reporting, operational tooling, and on-chain integrations.


If you love building in fast-moving environments, care deeply about data quality, and want to own core infrastructure at the heart of a modern fintech, this role is for you.


What You'll Do

Design, build, and maintain Rain’s core data pipelines, including ingestion from payments processors, card issuers, blockchain nodes, internal services, and third-party APIs.

Own orchestration and workflow management, implementing Airflow, Dagster, or similar tools to ensure reliable, observable, and scalable data processing.

Architect and manage Rain’s data warehouse (Snowflake, BigQuery, or Redshift), driving performance, cost optimization, partitioning, and access patterns.

Develop high-quality ELT/ETL transformations to structure raw logs, transactions, ledgers, and on-chain events into clean, production-grade datasets.

Implement data quality frameworks and observability (tests, data contracts, freshness checks, lineage) to ensure every dataset is trustworthy.

Partner closely with backend engineers to instrument new events, define data contracts, and improve telemetry across Rain’s infrastructure.

Support Analytics and cross-functional teams by delivering well-modeled, well-documented tables that power dashboards, ROI analyses, customer reporting, and key business metrics.

Own data reliability at scale, leading root-cause investigations, reducing pipeline failures, and building monitoring and alerting systems.

Evaluate and integrate new tools across ingestion, enrichment, observability, and developer experience—raising the bar on performance and maintainability.

Help set the long-term technical direction for Rain’s data platform as we scale across new products, regions, and chains.
jobsdetail.requirement:
Who You Are

Data infrastructure builder – You thrive in early-stage environments, owning pipelines and platforms end-to-end and choosing simplicity without sacrificing reliability.

Expert data engineer – Strong Python and SQL fundamentals, with real experience building production-grade ETL/ELT.

Workflow & orchestration fluent – Hands-on experience with Airflow, Dagster, Prefect, or similar systems.

Warehouse & modeling savvy – Comfortable designing schemas, optimizing performance, and operating modern cloud warehouses (Snowflake, BigQuery, Redshift).

Quality-obsessed – You care deeply about data integrity, testing, lineage, and observability.

Systems thinker – You see data as a platform; you design for reliability, scale, and future users.

Collaborator – You work well with backend engineers, analytics engineers, and cross-functional stakeholders to define requirements and deliver outcomes.

Experienced – 5–7+ years in data engineering roles, ideally within fintech, payments, B2B SaaS, or infrastructure-heavy startups.


Nice to Have

Experience ingesting and processing payments data, transaction logs, or ledger systems.

Exposure to smart contracts, blockchain data structures, or on-chain event ingestion.

Experience building data tooling for compliance, risk, or regulated environments.

Familiarity with dbt and/or semantic modeling to support analytics layers.

Prior experience standing up data platforms from 0→1 at early-stage companies.
jobsdetail.benefits:
Compensation & Package

Things that enable a fulfilling, healthy and happy experience at Rain:

Unlimited time off 🌴Unlimited vacation can be daunting, so we require Rainmakers to take 10 days minimum for themselves.

Flexible working ☕ We support a flexible workplace, if you feel comfortable at home please work from home. If you’d like to work with others in an office feel free to come in. We want everyone to be able to work in the environment in which they are their most confident and productive selves. New Rainmakers will have a stipend to create a comfortable atmosphere at home.

Easy to access benefits 🧠For US Rainmakers, we offer comprehensive health, dental and vision plans for you and your dependents, as well as a 100% company subsidized life insurance plan.

Retirement goals💡Plan for the future with confidence. We offer a 401(k) with a 4% company match.

Equity plan 📦 We offer every Rainmakers an equity option plan so we can all can benefit from our success.

Rain Cards 🌧️ We want Rainmakers to be knowledgeable about our core products and services. To support this mission, we issue a card for our team to utilize the card for testing.

Health and Wellness 📚 High performance begins from within. Rainmakers are welcome to use their card for eligible health and wellness spending like gym memberships/fitness classes, massages, acupuncture - whatever recharges you!

Team summits ✨ Summits play an important role at Rain! Time spent together helps us get to know each other, strengthen our relationships, and build a common destiny. Expect team and company offsites both domestically and internationally.


Interview Process

1.Recruiter / HR Call

Phone screen with the CTO

Looking at team fit, culture etc

2.Technical Interview

This will be diving deeper into past projects, impact they made etc.

3.Technical Assignment

2 hour session with the team. Half is architecture based and the other programming based.

4.Founder / CEO Interview

Last wrap-up call


About Rain

Rain has built a vertically integrated issuing stack focused on global services. Rain services platforms, banks, institutional custodians and everything in between. Our mission? To provide our partners with seamless access to products to service their global client base.

Company Size

11-50

Headquarters

US

Benefits

Company retreats

Equity / token allocation

Family care programs
