jobsdetail.requirement:
Role Overview

▪ Job Title: (Senior) Integration Specialist

▪ Department / Team: Technical Services / Customer Success Engineering

▪ Reason for Hiring: Backfill – previous employee exited due to poor performance and churned key accounts

▪ No. of Vacancies: 1


🌍 Location & Working Model

▪ Primary Location: Munich, Germany

▪ Remote / Hybrid / On-Site Policy: Hybrid or full on-site in Munich


🎯 Key Responsibilities

▪ Own end-to-end technical onboarding and integration for new customers, acting as primary contact and coordinator

▪ Execute web, shop, and CRM data integrations (JavaScript tagging, API / webhook setup, SQL checks)

▪ Provide 2nd-level technical support: issue reproduction, raw event analysis, troubleshooting before escalation to engineering

▪ Prepare solution concepts and feasibility assessments for custom client requests, incl. pricing input

▪ Liaise closely with Product, Sales, and Engineering to ensure seamless delivery and continuous improvement

▪ Transfer learnings across clients to optimise future implementations

▪ Maintain clear customer communication, documentation, and follow-ups throughout the integration lifecycle


✅ Ideal Candidate Profile

Must-Haves:

▪ Native German + English C1

▪ Strong JavaScript skills for tagging/event tracking

▪ Hands-on experience with website or shop integrations and analytics tools (e.g., Mixpanel, Adobe, GA)

▪ Proven work with APIs, webhooks, SQL, HTTP/cookies, event-driven analytics

▪ Track record of customer-facing technical onboarding or solution architecture

▪ Methodical, detail-oriented, able to transfer knowledge to new cases


Nice-to-Haves:

▪ Startup or fast-growth company background

▪ Python or other scripting for automation

▪ CRM system administration experience


❌ Dealbreakers / Red Flags:

▪ Low technical depth

▪ Frequent job changes

▪ No experience collaborating with engineering teams

▪ Unstructured or “feeler” personality types; lack of thoroughness

▪ Limited customer interaction history

Personality Traits:

▪ Preferred MBTI: INTJ, INTP, ISTJ (logical, systematic, self-directed)

▪ High sales empathy, customer centric, proactive problem solver


💚 Green Flags:

▪ Experience with website and/or shop integrations

▪ Heavy skilled in own Knowledge Transfer: Bestehendes Wissen auf neue Cases Transferieren (e.g. “hat bei dem Kunden so geklappt, könnten wir abgewandelt für siesen Kunden auch nutzen”)

▪ Experience with CRM Systems and data-driven environments: Weiß wie ein CRM funktioniert und worauf es in der Admin ankommt und hat ein Verständnis für die Relevanz von datengetriebenes Arbeiten

▪ Experience in a fast moving environment: Former companies and/or projects “startup-like”

▪ Experience in working with Engineering teams / technical background

▪ Experience with Tracking; BI and/or Performance Marketing

▪ Familiarity with analytics tools and data collection methods

▪ Strong understanding of JavaScript, especially for tagging and event tracking

▪ Ability to troubleshoot technical integration issues

▪ Experience with APIs, webhooks, and basic backend concepts

▪ SQL Skills: need to understand how to write database queries

▪ Knowledge of HTTP, cookies, and browser behavior

▪ Experience with Event-driven analytics systems

▪ Basic Scripting (e.g. Python)


🎓 Experience & Education

▪ Experience: Integration/implementation engineering, tracking analytics, solution architecture within analytics vendors or agencies

▪ Education: Degree in IT, Computer Science, or related field (beneficial)

▪ Specific Tools/Technologies: JavaScript, REST APIs, SQL, webhooks, HTTP, cookies, event-driven analytics platforms, CRM systems, basic Python
jobsdetail.benefits:
Compensation

▪ Base Salary Range: €55,000 – €70,000 gross p.a.


📄 Contract Details

▪ Contract Type: Permanent, full-time (Festanstellung, Vollzeit)

▪ Probation period: 6 months

▪ Vacation: 24 days


🎁 Perks

▪ Flat hierarchies & fast decision cycles

▪ Technically challenging, performance-focused environment

▪ Culture of continuous learning & modern tech adoption


🧭 Sourcing Guidance (Recruiter Enablement)

▪ Target Industries / Companies (Whitelist): Web analytics vendors (Mixpanel, Adobe, Google Analytics, Contentsquare, Piwik PRO); tracking/attribution SaaS; analytics implementation agencies; MarTech consultancies

▪ Avoided Companies / Backgrounds (Blacklist): Non-technical roles, pure marketing with no integration exposure, job-hoppers

▪ Relevant Job Titles: Integration Specialist, Implementation Engineer, Technical Consultant, Solution Architect, Tracking Engineer, Customer Success Engineer, Technical Onboarding Manager

▪ Boolean Search Suggestion: ("Integration Specialist" OR "Implementation Engineer" OR "Solution Architect" OR "Tracking Consultant") AND (JavaScript OR "event tracking") AND (API OR webhook) AND (German) AND (Munich OR Bayern)

▪ Pitch Ideas for Candidate Outreach: Emphasise high ownership of client integrations, hybrid work in Munich, exposure to cutting-edge event-driven analytics, clear career impact on customer success, and a culture that values logical thinkers and technical depth


📈 KPIs & Success Metrics

▪ Successful integrations completed on time

▪ Reduction in technical onboarding duration

▪ Customer satisfaction / NPS post-integration

▪ Decrease in 2nd-level support escalations

▪ Retention of key accounts post-go-live


🔎 Hiring Process

▪ Step 1 – 10-min scheduling / pre-screen call with MSC

▪ Step 2 – Screening & first interview with Moritz (recommendation) → Slack decision by Adam/Dennis

▪ Step 3 – Leader Call with Dennis (customer-related fit and Tracify pitch)

▪ Step 4 – Specialist Call with Adam (deep technical assessment)

▪ Step 5 – Informal dinner with Dennis, Marius, Adam/Moritz
jobsdetail.other:
⚡ Quick Start for Experienced Recruiters

1) Review the must-haves and sourcing guidance thoroughly – be selective from the start.

2) Prioritize outreach to profiles with the highest match potential, even if they are passive.

3) Leverage niche networks and competitor talent pools before going broad.

4) Use the pitch ideas to create a compelling, personalized opening message – avoid generic outreach.

5) Aim to submit your top 2–3 strongest profiles early, to set the tone with quality.

6) Keep juucy updated on market feedback, objections, and talent availability trends.


Extra Recruiting Requirement – Special Requirement

According to the MBTI personality test, candidates must have one of the following personality types to be considered for this role:

▪ Architect (INTJ)

▪ Logician (INTP)

▪ Logistician (ISTJ)

➡️ Please share the MBTI test with your candidates and include the test results in the Briefing Notes.

Test link: https://www.16personalities.com/de/kostenloser-personlichkeitstest

🚨 IMPORTANT: Please do NOT disclose to candidates which personality types Tracify is specifically looking for.

jobsdetail.description:
New role live with Raiku! Candidates must be Solana native and worked on runtime, scheduler or other core systems on Solana - please ensure that the candidate githubs are active


About Raiku

Raiku is reengineering blockchain infrastructure from first principles to make global digital markets as precise and dependable as physical systems. Built on Solana, our platform delivers deterministic execution, guaranteed inclusion, and low-latency performance—solving the foundational failures that cause transactions to miss, trades to revert, and systems to collapse under pressure. By placing high-performance compute close to where transactions happen and coordinating execution through our advanced scheduling engine, Raiku empowers developers to build scalable, high-performance applications—and gives institutions the reliability and control they demand. We believe financial infrastructure should behave like physics: fast, reliable, and predictable—every time, without exception.


The Role

As a Senior Validator Engineer at Raiku, you will spearhead our engineering efforts surrounding the Solana Validator (both Agave and Firedancer), driving the innovation and implementation of next-generation high-performance distributed systems. You will work with a highly proficient team of core engineers who have all contributed to foundational and novel network components.


We believe in hiring only exceptional individuals who are highly motivated to work on complex core infrastructure challenges and are motivated by a coherent system design philosophy that will drive our industry forward. Expect frequent group discussions on architecture, new design specs and code reviews. We are all deeply committed to frequently shipping quality code.


About the Engineering Team

As a team, we are building an entire platform written in Rust, which connects the L1 and L2s and allows us to create deeply complex scenarios and interactions with the running network. While your primary focus for this role is Rust and complex interactions with different components, you may also be involved with other types of development, including exposure to the C-coded Firedancer validator. Working at every level of the stack is necessary to understand the big picture and how everything is wired together.


Responsibilities:

Highly motivated to contribute to our mission and be part of something bigger. Excited to work on projects that are ground-breaking and complex

Refactor, improve and lead software design and implementation

Communicate effectively with the team and document your code. It is also expected that senior engineers mentor less experienced engineers.

Read and understand protocol specifications and be able to break them into issues and turn them into code.

Maintaining a large codebase with many components and keeping it well-designed, future-proofed, modular and highly performant

Automate security testing processes and benchmarks, creating innovative tools and frameworks for continuously improving our systems.
jobsdetail.requirement:
What You'll Bring:

A bachelor's or master's degree in Computer Science, Engineering, or a related technical field, coupled with practical experience in blockchain systems.

At least 3 years of experience in blockchain and distributed systems, with a deep technical understanding of L1 and L2 architectures. Ideally Solana.

Experience in writing efficient low-level code.

Strong proficiency in Rust, with at least 2-3 years of Rust experience.

Demonstrated experience in designing, developing, and deploying scalable distributed systems.

An analytical mindset with the ability to anticipate and mitigate emerging security threats, leveraging a comprehensive understanding of the blockchain landscape.

Direct involvement in the Solana ecosystem, with contributions to Solana's runtime, scheduler, or other core systems.


Preferred Qualifications:

Proficiency and experience in writing C code.

Active open-source contributions in core protocol engineering (such as Firedancer, Reth, Lighthouse, Geth, …).

Active engagement with the blockchain security research community, evidenced by contributions to open-source projects, publications, or presentations at notable conferences.


About Raiku

Raiku reimagines how applications interact with Solana through infrastructure products that improve scalability, reliability and performance.

Resources

Website

https://www.raiku.com/

Twitter

https://x.com/raikucom

jobsdetail.description:
Description

About us


Defuse Labs develops NEAR Intents to enable seamless cross-chain interactions in an automated world — connecting AI, services, and financial applications. At Defuse Labs we design intent-driven liquidity layers, where smart contracts breathe, AI transacts and chains sync in real-time With expertise in AI, cryptography, and decentralized finance, our team is redefining how intelligent agents interact across blockchain networks.


We invite you to be part of the team to support development and further maintenance of Near Intents. As a key player in the industry, Near Intents fosters a culture of excellence, collaboration, and forward-thinking. We are operating in a startup mode that values creativity, agility, and a strong sense of purpose. The mission is to build a decentralized future where financial systems are open, transparent, and accessible to everyone.


We are looking for a Partner Engineer (DevRel) to drive adoption, manage strategic partnerships, and engage with the developer ecosystem around NearIntents. This role blends hands-on engineering expertise with developer advocacy, requiring a deep technical understanding of the NEAR ecosystem and the ability to support developers across multiple channels. You will be the Point of Contact (PoC) for integrations, ensuring developers and users can easily leverage Near Intents in their applications.


Key Responsibilities


Integrations & Adoption

Drive the adoption of Near Intents by working closely with partners and developers to integrate it into their applications.

Work closely with developers to integrate Near Intents, solving technical challenges and optimizing implementations.

Write high-quality technical documentation, tutorials, and example repositories; create technical resources (guides, tutorials, SDKs) to accelerate integrations.

Debug and troubleshoot issues, providing direct engineering support to partners and ecosystem projects.

Identify and address technical barriers, ensuring smooth implementation across various projects.


Developer Support & Advocacy

Serve as the primary Point of Contact (PoC) for partners and users, answering technical inquiries and troubleshooting issues.

Actively support and engage with developers across Telegram, GitHub, Twitter, and forums to provide real-time assistance.

Advocate for developer needs internally, ensuring Near Intents evolves in alignment with community feedback.


Technical Expertise & Enablement

Maintain a deep understanding of Near Intents and its underlying technology to effectively assist developers.

Conduct workshops, AMAs, and office hours to educate and empower the community.

Develop example use cases, reference implementations, and best practices to showcase the power of Near Intents.


Ecosystem Engagement & Growth

Collaborate with ecosystem projects, tooling providers, and infrastructure teams to expand the reach of Near Intents.

Build relationships with key developers, fostering a strong technical community around Near Intents.

Monitor adoption metrics and provide insights to continuously improve developer experience.

Who We Are:

We build infrastructure that delivers massive amounts of web data to the companies training the world’s most powerful AI models.

We're the team that helps to power and support Grass, a bandwidth-sharing network that lets us operate a massive distributed crawler, giving us unique access to high-quality public web data at global scale. On top of that, we’ve built pipelines for ingesting, segmenting, and annotating billions of videos, transcripts, and audio files, powering dataset creation for frontier labs.

We’re lean, technical, and move fast. No red tape, no slow decision-making; just a team of builders pushing to expand what’s possible for open web data and AI.


The Role.

We are looking for a Embedded Systems Engineer who is experienced and brings deep expertise in backend and firmware development. You will join a small, talented team and lead efforts to architect scalable solutions, build and optimize low-level systems, drive technical excellence, and support our vision for a future where Grass plays a critical role in shaping the internet's evolution.


Who You Are.

BS or MS degree in a STEM major or equivalent job experience required

4+ years experience in software development and writing very high quality code that is robust and easy to maintain

Knowledge on how to build large scalable systems

Experience with low-level programming, embedded systems, or firmware development for networked devices

Knowledge and experience in identifying and solving issues with intricate, live software systems, as well as in-depth familiarity with contemporary software development processes such as continuous integration and continuous deployment

Professional or native English language proficiency

About Grass

Grass is the world’s first Layer 2 Data Rollup. It exists as a source for AI training data, which it obtains from public websites using a vast network of web scraping nodes. Data procured on the network is then cleaned and processed into structured datasets before being added to Grass’s growing data repository for use in AI training.

Description

Head of Marketing

Craft the Story of How Ethereum Wins Institutional Adoption


Why Etherealize?

Etherealize is bringing the real world to Ethereum. We are building the infrastructure for Wall Street to operate on Ethereum-based rails and move the financial system into the 21st century.


We are looking for a visionary marketing leader to help us revolutionize financial markets with next-generation technology. You will be joining a team dedicated to unlocking the true potential of on-chain finance, creating a world where traditional assets flow seamlessly through blockchain protocols.


Etherealize has a series of exciting products that will be revealed in 2026, and we need the right person to help amplify them to the crypto community, to Wall Street, and to the world.


What Will You Do?

You will report directly to the CEO and own the marketing function, driving strategy and execution to position Etherealize as the bridge between institutional finance and Ethereum.


Strategy & Narrative

Craft narratives for two distinct audiences:

Wall Street (credit market agents, investment executives)

Web3 (crypto-natives, technologists)

Marketing artifacts supporting the launch of the institutional protocol

comments.text:
Hiring Criteria
Has already put an autonomous learning system into production that directly controlled capital, pricing, traffic, or resources and can explain what broke and how they fixed it.

Has personally designed and enforced hard risk limits (capital caps, loss bounds, circuit breakers) in a live system, not just talked about “risk-aware objectives.”

Has built a policy evaluation loop from scratch (simulators, replay, counterfactuals, shadow deployments) before trusting live rollout.

Can make and defend uncomfortable tradeoffs (e.g. heuristic > RL, bandit > deep RL) based on empirical results instead of ideology.

Has operated as the single owner of a complex ML system in a small team, with no safety net of research orgs, infra teams, or “ML platforms.”

jobsdetail.description:
Description

Baton Corporation is a team of elite builders moving fast and redefining what’s possible. We’re a high-growth software development company and proud contributors to pump.fun, the largest crypto social network in the world.

We build products at the frontier of crypto, consumer scale, and performance. Our work ships to millions of users, operates under real-world constraints, and sets the bar for speed, polish, and reliability in the space.

We are seeking a Chief Financial Officer (CFO) to take command of our financial engine. As a core member of the executive team, you will manage massive liquidity, optimize high-volume revenue streams, and navigate the complex intersection of social media and decentralised finance.


Key Responsibilities

Hyper-Growth Financial Strategy: Drive financial operations for a high-revenue platform, focusing on liquidity management, aggressive scaling, and real-time risk mitigation in the volatile memecoin market.

Treasury & Revenue Optimisation: Oversee the management of $800M+ in revenue, ensuring optimal cash positioning, yield strategies, and secure custody across fiat and digital assets.

Solana Ecosystem Integration: Optimize crypto payment processing, exchange integrations, and liquidity routing specific to the Solana network and pump.fun ecosystem.

Regulatory & Tax Navigation: Own the strategy for complex global tax obligations and compliance challenges unique to high-velocity crypto social platforms.

Executive Relationship Management: Maintain high-level relationships with account managers at banks, payment processors, and Tier-1 crypto exchanges to ensure seamless operations.

Hands-on Financial Controls: Implement robust reporting structures and policies from the ground up, moving fast from "startup mode" to institutional-grade financial governance.

Team Building: Recruit and lead a high-performing finance team that can keep pace with our elite engineering and product squads.
jobsdetail.requirement:
Requirements

Experience: 10+ years in finance, with at least 3+ years in a senior leadership role specifically within a high-volume crypto environment (Exchange, DeFi protocol, or top-tier Trading Firm).

Solana & Crypto Native: Deep understanding of the Solana ecosystem, digital asset management, and the transaction lifecycles of high-frequency social/trading platforms.

Operational Grit: Proven ability to "grind"—you are as comfortable building a 5-year strategic model as you are performing manual reconciliation or grunt work to ensure accuracy.

The "Elite Builder" Mindset: Experience in hyper-growth tech where speed is a competitive advantage; you must be able to drive financial insights that support split-second executive decisions.

Regulatory Mastery: Strong grasp of the evolving global regulatory landscape for tokens, social finance (SocialFi), and crypto-native revenue models.

jobsdetail.description:
New role with Sei Labs - this is an urgent requirement! They're looking for more junior profiles who can work onsite with 1-4 years experience.


Description

About Sei Labs

Sei Labs builds open sourced technology for the high-performance Sei Blockchain, the first parallelized EVM Layer 1 blockchain designed to scale with the industry. The unique optimizations built into Sei enable Web3 applications to reach Web2 level scale and performance, driving the mass adoption of digital assets.

Our teams are comprised of former Google, Goldman Sachs, Robinhood, Nvidia, and Databricks veterans dedicated to onboarding the next billion users onto a vibrant ecosystem of applications.

Sei Labs has raised over $30M from top investors including Jump Crypto, Multicoin Capital, Coinbase Ventures, Distributed Global, Hudson River Trading, and Flow Traders.

Check out our website and docs for more details.


Recruitment at Sei Labs

We’re looking to hire our first Sourcer / Talent Acquisition Specialist to help us build the most talent dense Engineering team in web3. This role will report directly into our Head of Talent. It is the perfect learning ground for someone early in their recruiting career who is ambitious and hungry to grow, with a strong bias toward action and ownership.

You’ll have an incredible opportunity to work directly with our founders and leadership team to shape hiring strategy for some of the most critical roles at Sei. You’ll spend the majority of your time sourcing and engaging the pinnacle of web3 engineering talent for highly technical roles, while also contributing to longer-term talent strategy, market mapping, recruiting operations, and wider HR work.

This is not a passive recruiting role. It’s for someone who thrives in fast-moving high-autonomy environments, and wants real responsibility.


Responsibilities

Proactively attract, network with, and source the top decile of tech + non-tech talent in web3

Own and strategize how to build a consistent top-of-funnel pipeline for our toughest and most high impact searches

Partner directly with our Founder and Engineering Leaders on market mapping, talent strategy, and hiring priorities

Contribute to wider HR work, recruiting operations, tooling, and automation projects as we scale
jobsdetail.requirement:
Requirements

Strong learning mindset and proven enthusiasm for Web3

1–4 years of experience in sourcing talent, with exposure to technical or engineering roles

At least 1 years of experience in web3 specific talent pools or crypto adjacent companies (CEX’s / blockchain analytics / etc.)

Proven ability to run outbound sourcing end-to-end and manage multiple pipelines simultaneously

Strong written communication skills and comfort engaging highly technical candidates

High grit, resilience, and comfort operating in a fast-paced startup environment

About the Role

We’re looking for a Product Designer to help bring early-stage product ideas and new initiatives to life. This role sits at the intersection of brand, product, and marketing, with a focus on defining the first visual expression of new ideas—from landing pages and launch moments to early product visuals.

You’ll work closely with a small, fast-moving team to shape how new projects look, feel, and communicate value from day one. This is a hands-on role for a designer with strong visual taste, comfort with ambiguity, and excitement about building the first version of things.


What You’ll Do

Help define the visual direction for new initiatives and early-stage projects, including brand identity, look & feel, and storytelling.

Design early landing pages, launch assets, and marketing surfaces to support new ideas as they come to market.

Own end-to-end visual design projects, from early concepts through polished execution and launch.

Collaborate closely with design, product, engineering, marketing, and leadership partners to turn loose ideas into clear, compelling visuals.

Contribute visual design and polish to product experiences as projects evolve.

Maintain a high bar for craft, detail, and clarity across everything you ship.

Experiment with and incorporate AI and emerging tools to improve ideation, iteration speed, and production quality.

Balance creative exploration with practical execution in a fast-moving environment.
jobsdetail.requirement:
What we’re looking for

3+ years of professional design experience, with the ability to independently own visual design projects.

Strong foundation in typography, layout, color, and composition.

Experience designing for digital surfaces such as web or product interfaces.

Proficiency with modern design tools (e.g. Figma).

Ability to context switch working across multiple 0-1 brands simultaneously.

Interest in experimenting with AI tools to enhance your design process (prior experience is a plus, but not required).

A strong portfolio demonstrating:

Visual design excellence across brand, product, or marketing work

Thoughtful storytelling and range

High-quality execution and attention to detail


Nice to Have

Experience working on early-stage products or new initiatives.

Startup or fast-paced team experience.

Motion, illustration, or 3D skills.

Familiarity with or curiosity about web3 products and ecosystems.

Experience collaborating on product UI or product-adjacent design work.

Example Projects You’ll Own

Defining the visual direction for a new initiative or early-stage product.

Designing and shipping a first-pass landing page for a new launch.

Visual exploration and concepting for new ideas before they’re fully formed.

Supporting visual design and polish for early product experiences as needed.

jobsdetail.description:
About the Role

As a Senior Data Engineer, you’ll own the reliability, quality, and performance of Stan’s data pipelines and integrations. You’ll build and maintain the data infrastructure that powers analytics, decision-making, and AI products used by thousands of creators. This role is ideal for someone who enjoys operating under real ownership — keeping systems running, fixing what breaks, and designing infrastructure that scales.


Key Responsibilities

Own the health, reliability, and performance of production data pipelines across the company.

Build, maintain, and debug ETL/ELT pipelines feeding analytics, AI systems, and internal tools.

Ensure data quality, monitoring, and alerting so issues are caught early and fixed fast.

Design and maintain integrations across the data stack (APIs, warehouses, orchestration, downstream consumers).

Support and extend AI and agentic systems by building robust data and context pipelines.

Partner closely with data science, product, and engineering to enable data-driven decision-making.

Proactively improve infrastructure to prevent failures and scale with the business.
jobsdetail.requirement:
Required

3+ years of professional experience in data engineering or analytics engineering.

Strong Python and SQL skills (non-negotiable).

Hands-on experience building and owning production ETL/ELT pipelines.

Experience with cloud data warehouses (Snowflake or similar).

Familiarity with orchestration tools (Airflow or equivalent).

Experience implementing data quality checks, monitoring, and alerting.

Comfort debugging complex data issues in production environments.

Strong communication skills and ability to partner with non-data stakeholders.


Nice to have

Experience with AWS (ECS, Lambdas, RDS).

Hands-on work with Airbyte, DBT, or similar tools.

Experience building API integrations or scraping pipelines.

Exposure to LLMs, agentic systems, or AI data pipelines.

Experience supporting early-stage or fast-growing startups.


Quick self-check


If you’ve owned pipelines end-to-end and been responsible when things break, you’re likely a strong fit.

Stan is the Shopify for content creators, helping anyone make a living working for themselves online. Backed by top-tier investors and builders, Stan is scaling fast — with data at the center of how decisions get made.

Learn more: https://www.stan.store/

About the Role

As a UX Researcher at Stan, you’ll shape the product by deeply understanding creators at every stage of their journey and translating their needs into clear, actionable insights. You’ll plan and run qualitative and quantitative research, synthesize complex signals into patterns, and partner closely with Design, Product, Engineering, and Data to influence real product decisions. This is a high-impact role for a researcher who cares about craft, collaboration, and ensuring human insight sits at the center of a fast-growing creator platform.

Key responsibilities

Watch user sessions and interpret and gather insights and formulating into patterns

Plan and conduct qualitative and quantitative user research across the Creator lifecycle

Run user interviews, usability testing, and concept validation to inform product direction

Analyze user behavior through session reviews, surveys, and data synthesis

Translate research findings into clear insights, opportunity areas, and actionable recommendations

Partner closely with Design, Product, Engineering, and Data to influence roadmap and priorities

Create research plans, discussion guides, and reports that scale knowledge across teams

Advocate for creator needs and ensure user evidence is central to decision-making

jobsdetail.description:
About the Role

This is a Forward Deployment Engineer role for someone 

who likes being close to customers, products, and real-world outcomes. 

You’ll sit at the intersection of product launches, client implementations, 

and internal teams, making sure new features actually land well and deliver value.

You’ll work closely with customers, support, AI operations, product, and engineering to launch products, troubleshoot issues, and close the feedback loop between what clients need and what gets built next.

This is not a narrow role. It’s hands-on, fast-moving, and high ownership.

Key responsibilities

What You’ll Do

Program manage new product launches, including internal knowledge sessions, implementation training, and enablement materials

Use SQL and BI tools to monitor product health post-launch and partner with Support and AI Ops to fix misconfigurations

Own feedback loops by synthesizing insights from Gong calls, support tickets, surveys, and other signals

Act as the voice of the customer and help shape short- and long-term product decisions

Partner closely with Product and Engineering to improve offerings and ensure real client impact

Run discovery sessions with new customers to understand workflows and requirements

Move fast, solve real problems, and build systems that scale

Description

About Sei Labs

Sei Labs builds open sourced technology for the high-performance Sei Blockchain, the first parallelized EVM Layer 1 blockchain designed to scale with the industry. The unique optimizations built into Sei enable Web3 applications to reach Web2 level scale and performance, driving the mass adoption of digital assets.

Our teams are comprised of former Google, Goldman Sachs, Robinhood, Nvidia, and Databricks veterans dedicated to onboarding the next billion users onto a vibrant ecosystem of applications.

Sei Labs has raised over $30M from top investors including Jump Crypto, Multicoin Capital, Coinbase Ventures, Distributed Global, Hudson River Trading, and Flow Traders.

Check out our website and docs for more details.

Incubations Engineering at Sei

Sei Labs is looking for an Engineering Manager to help shape the future of Web3 by empowering innovative, high-impact applications across the Sei Blockchain. You'll lead a team at the forefront of blockchain technology, collaborating closely with our ecosystem partners to develop cutting-edge dApps spanning diverse Web3 domains (from DEX’s and games, to DePin, AI and beyond). If you're passionate about constant learning, rapid development, and making a tangible impact in a fast-paced, community-first environment, this is a unique opportunity to push the boundaries of what's possible in decentralized technology.

Terraform-first infrastructure with reusable modules and policy guardrails, plus secure CI/CD pipelines (blue/green or canary deployments, rollback strategies). Experience in regulated or security-focused environments is required or strongly preferred (please specify frameworks if applicable).

title: Technical Product Manager, Data Center at  Meter

jobsdetail.description:
About the Role

This role will help launch Meter’s Data Center product line, scoping and driving requirements across hardware, firmware, and software. You’ll work closely with Meter’s founders and engineering leadership to accelerate product velocity, reduce decision fatigue, and prevent costly execution traps. Success is measured by shipping high-quality TOR and spine switching products that deploy smoothly to real customers.

Meter is offering visa sponsorship for this role.

Key responsibilities

Youʼll own everything from considering hardware tradeoffs like component quality and placement for desired cost and performance, to firmware implications like control plane feature density and its resource availability, to the design of an API.

You'll partner with engineering team leads and senior engineers to bring proactive clarity to product development, while constantly learning and bringing these industry learnings to Meter.

Great work happens through sheer perseverance and doing the basics really well (e.g., speaking to one more potential customer, running one more test, asking why one more time, etc.).

title: Senior Solutions Engineer – Integrations & AI Deployments - Arist

jobsdetail.description:
Job Overview

As a Senior Solutions Engineer focused on Integrations & AI Deployments, you will play a pivotal role in designing and implementing complex customer integrations across systems like HCM, LMS, and CRM. With a strong background in working with REST APIs, webhooks, data pipelines, and integration platforms such as Workato, you'll ensure deployments are not only reliable and secure but also scalable and maintainable. Acting as the technical lead for high-impact enterprise go-lives, you'll expand and strengthen our platform's APIs, developing repeatable, template-based integrations. This role requires you to define integration patterns, develop tooling, maintain documentation, and establish best practices for quicker deployment using reusable frameworks. Additionally, you will partner with our product and engineering teams to influence key roadmap decisions. In collaboration with enterprise customers, you will deploy AI-powered needs analysis, mapping required datasets and validating data quality while designing guardrails for AI outputs to ensure accuracy and compliance. Your implementations will seamlessly integrate into each organization, reflecting your ability to deliver native-feeling systems. With a base in the US and work authorization, you should have over six years of experience in Solutions Engineering or similar roles, and a proven track record of end-to-end ownership of complex integrations. Strong API and data pipeline expertise are crucial, complemented by your ability to communicate effectively with both technical and non-technical stakeholders. With a compensation range of $200,000 to $220,000 per year, this role presents an exciting opportunity to contribute to and grow within a high-growth AI startup.

Responsibilities

1. Own Complex Customer Integrations

Design and implement integrations across HCM, LMS, CRM, and related enterprise systems

Work with REST APIs, webhooks, data pipelines, and integration platforms (e.g., Workato)

Ensure deployments are reliable, secure, scalable, and easy for customers to maintain

Act as technical lead for high-impact enterprise go-lives

2. Build & Productize Arist’s Integration Surface

Expand and harden Arist’s APIs to support repeatable, template-based integrations

Define integration patterns, tooling, documentation, and best practices

Reduce time-to-deployment through reusable frameworks

Partner with product and core engineering to influence roadmap decisions

3. Deploy AI Needs Analysis in Real Environments

Work directly with enterprise customers to deploy AI-powered needs analysis

Map required datasets and validate data quality

Design guardrails for AI outputs (accuracy, safety, compliance)

Deliver implementations that feel native inside each organization
jobsdetail.requirement:
Qualifications

Solutions & Integration Experience

6+ years of professional experience in Solutions Engineering, Forward Deployed Engineering, or similar customer-facing technical roles

Proven track record owning complex integrations end-to-end

Experience with APIs, webhooks, ETL/data pipelines, and integration platforms

Technical Strength

Strong understanding of REST APIs, authentication (OAuth), and enterprise SaaS architectures

Experience with systems like HCM, LMS, CRM platforms

Familiarity with Rails is a plus (not required)

Comfort working with AI/LLM-based systems

Customer & Cross-Functional Ability

Strong communicator across technical and non-technical stakeholders

Experience collaborating with product, engineering, sales, and customer success

Able to balance tactical execution with long-term scalability

Ideal Candidate

Senior solutions engineer who thrives on complex customer deployments

Equally comfortable reading API docs and presenting to CIOs

Enjoys turning bespoke implementations into scalable systems

Motivated by revenue, retention, and adoption — not just technical output

Excited to help define the Solutions Engineering function at a high-growth AI startup

Relocation & Sponsorship

X Relocation Assistance

X Visa Sponsorship
jobsdetail.other:
Additional Information

This is a high-visibility, customer-facing role where you’ll work directly with enterprise clients to deploy and shape cutting-edge AI systems in real environments. You won’t just “support integrations” — you’ll own deployments that directly impact revenue, retention, and product adoption.

Arist is at an inflection point. The platform is already trusted by major global organizations, and demand for AI-powered enablement is accelerating. We are investing heavily in making our integrations and AI needs-analysis systems more scalable, repeatable, and enterprise-ready — and this role is central to that effort.

You will operate with significant autonomy and ownership. We value engineers who think in systems, write clearly, move quickly, and take responsibility for outcomes — not just output.

We are actively interviewing and will move quickly for strong candidates.

There's been an important update regarding the Senior Solutions Engineer – Integrations & AI Deployments role with Arist:

We’re looking for a strong, hands-on Solutions Engineer who leans more toward real implementation than sales engineering, with proven experience owning and shipping production-grade API integrations end to end, repeatedly standing up the same integrations across multiple customers, and optimizing for long-term stability, reliability, and maintainability, not just quick PoCs or prototypes; the ideal profile comes from enterprise B2B SaaS, is comfortable working closely with customers on integrations without becoming a project manager, and can move fast to implement, debug, and harden integrations in real-world environments.

jobsdetail.description:
About the Role

This is a rare opportunity to step in as Shoplift’s first VP of Product and own the full product function end-to-end — strategy, roadmap, team, and outcomes. You’ll help scale the company from founder-led product motion into a disciplined, high-performing product organization, defining what “self-optimizing storefronts” means for modern ecommerce. The mandate is clear: build the systems, team, and product-led growth engine that drives retention, expansion, and category leadership.

Shoplift does not offer visa sponsorship at this time.

🔑 Key responsibilities

Product Strategy, Vision, & Direction

Define and communicate a clear long-term product vision aligned with company strategy. Set direction for what we build, why it matters, and how it differentiates us in the market. Translate strategy into a coherent roadmap that balances innovation, merchant needs, and business priorities. Ensure all product teams are aligned around a shared understanding of goals, success measures, and tradeoffs.

Product Organization Leadership

Build, scale, and lead the entire product function—not just PMs, but the systems, rituals, and processes that make product excellent. Establish hiring philosophy, planning cadence, decision frameworks, and accountability mechanisms.

Product Management

Lead the end-to-end lifecycle of the product from roadmap definition to delivery and iteration. Ensure high-quality execution, reliable delivery, and measurable adoption of new capabilities. Balance innovation and operational excellence by prioritizing impact, speed, and quality in all releases. Establish clear product process: how we prioritize, how decisions get made, what "definition of done" means. Make hard tradeoffs between new capabilities and technical debt.

Product Positioning

Own how the product competes and wins in the market. Conduct ongoing market research to understand the competitive landscape, platform trends, feature differentiation, and pricing models. Define and evolve pricing and packaging strategies that align with merchant value, product usage patterns, and business growth goals. Ensure the product’s value proposition, positioning, and monetization model are cohesive and inform go-to-market alignment.

Product Analytics

Define how success is measured for every product initiative. Ensure product analytics systems are instrumented correctly and insights flow into decision-making. Partner with Data and Growth to quantify product performance, usage patterns, and merchant impact.

Product Merchandising

Ensure the right features are discoverable, understood, and adopted. Drive in-product communication, feature activation, and cross-sell/upsell experiences that surface value to merchant at the right time.

Product-Led Growth (PLG)

Build self-serve growth loops into the product experience. Design shareable, viral, or usage-based mechanisms (e.g., shareable reports, feature unlocks) that drive organic adoption and expansion. Collaborate with Marketing and Growth to align PLG efforts with overall revenue strategy.

Product Documentation

Own the quality and completeness of product guides, tutorials, and help content. Ensure documentation is clear, up to date, and supports both onboarding and self-serve merchants.

Product Stickiness

Drive product stickiness and expansion through value realization and engagement. Design experiences that make the product indispensable increasing usage depth and multi-feature adoption. Partner with Growth Teams to identify product signals of expansion or churn risk, and prioritize roadmap initiatives that strengthen NDR.

Customer Empathy

Own our customer feedback loops. Ensure every feature is grounded in deep understanding of how ecommerce operators accelerate their success—their workflows, pain points, friction, and aspirations.

Support Experience & Systems

Own the support experience as an internal product: scoped, prioritized, and maintained with rigor. Partner with Engineering & Customer Success to ensure systems and processes meet internal SLAs. Build clear visibility into support data and patterns so product teams can identify recurring issues, quantify friction, and prioritize fixes or improvements in the roadmap.

Cross-Functional Partnership

Work closely with Engineering, Success, Sales, and Marketing. Partner with co-founder Jason Boit (Head of Product Experience) to ensure design and architecture amplify product strategy.

Metrics & Outcomes

Own key business outcomes: retention, engagement depth, net dollar retention. Connect product bets to measurable impact.
jobsdetail.benefits:
About the Company

Ecommerce brands face rising acquisition costs and intense competition, yet many still make key merchandising and pricing decisions without testing what actually converts. The gap between guessing and knowing can drive 15–30% differences in revenue, but traditional A/B testing has been too slow or complex for most merchants.

Shoplift is the fastest, easiest A/B testing platform built specifically for Shopify. They help brands quickly identify what drives conversion using visual editors, AI-powered recommendations, and expert guidance.

Trusted by 2,000+ brands—from high-growth DTC companies to Shopify Plus merchants—Shoplift pairs a powerful product with deep Shopify partnerships and a Customer Success team focused on delivering real business outcomes.

Shoplift is built by operators who value speed, ownership, and execution. They move fast, iterate often, and expect work that directly impacts revenue.

🌐 Company website

https://shoplift.ai

title: Senior Backend Engineer - Stellar Ecosystem -LI.FI
location: Remote - EMEA / EU Hours - Authorised to work in EMEA

jobsdetail.description:
Important: Please read the following before referring candidates

They are hiring remotely across EMEA but potentially open to other locations, provided candidates are willing to work overlapping hours with the EU (this is a must).

Previous experience in Web3/DeFi is required developers

Li.Fi is simplifying multi-chain DeFi for traditional financial institutions, fintechs and service providers by offering a unified API to access all on-chain liquidity across major blockchains for best price same-chain and cross-chain swaps. We are a passionate team on a mission to enable a fairer, more decentralised world. Apart from the tech, we care a lot about user experience and aim to make complex technologies accessible to everyone.

A quick run-down of the job

We are looking for multiple Senior Backend Engineers who will lead the design, development, and maintenance of our backend systems, ensuring they are robust, scalable, and secure. You'll collaborate closely with cross-functional teams, including frontend developers, product managers, and designers, to deliver high-quality software solutions that drive our success. The position will report to our Backend Lead.

The current tech stack of LI.FI utilizes:

Node.js running TypeScript code in Docker containers

MongoDB and Redis for our data

ethers.js for communication with the blockchains

Github, Kubernetes, AWS and CloudFlare for our GitOps CD chain

Please note: You must be located in the EMEA or APAC regions and have technical understanding of blockchain. Your application will not be considered otherwise.

What you'll do

Leading and actively participating in the design, development, and maintenance of backend systems and APIs

Architecting and implementing scalable and robust backend solutions that can handle growing user loads and evolving business needs

Maintaining high coding standards, conduct code reviews, and ensuring best practices for code maintainability and readability

Identifying bottlenecks and optimizing code and algorithms to improve system performance

Implementing monitoring and debugging tools to proactively identify and resolve issues, ensuring high system availability

Developing and maintaining comprehensive test suites, including unit tests, integration tests, and end-to-end tests, to ensure the reliability of backend systems

Providing guidance and mentorship to junior developers, helping them grow their technical skills

Analyzing complex technical problems and propose effective solutions to address them

Documenting code, APIs, and technical processes to facilitate knowledge sharing within the team and support onboarding of new developers
jobsdetail.requirement:
What makes a good candidate

Must be located in the EMEA region

Strong experience with TypeScript is required

Experience working with the Stellar network in production environments

Previous experience in the blockchain space, with a technical understanding of the EVM ecosystem (or other blockchain ecosystems), is a plus

Extensive professional experience in a similar role (senior or lead level)

Solid understanding of document databases and caching mechanisms

Experience in software design and system architecture

Strong knowledge of testing frameworks and best practices

Excellent communication, collaboration, problem-solving, and analytical skills

Proactive mindset with the ability to work autonomously and collaboratively

Ability to write maintainable, scalable, production-grade code

Curiosity for new technologies and the ability to evaluate and suggest improvements independently

Very strong written and verbal English skills for effective communication with team members and stakeholders

Experience with Stellar’s Soroban smart contracts is a plus

Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Computer Science is a plus
jobsdetail.benefits:
Company culture

Embracing the differences

Embrace your own differences

Respect and welcome the unique qualities of others

See others’ differences as an opportunity to grow

Own it, do it

Own tasks end-to-end

Hold yourself to a high bar for execution

Make bold decisions, and be accountable

Understand your limitations and seek for help

Fail forward

Try - don’t be afraid to fail

Learn - don’t make the same mistake twice

Adapt - grow with the experience

Have fun together

Take the time to get to know and understand each other better

Don’t take yourself too seriously and keep it light-hearted

Fun should be coming from a place of love and respect

What you’ll get

Annual team offsites (Thailand in 2023 and Vietnam in 2024 were a blast, Marrakech in March 2025 was blast-ier; let’s see what happens in 2026!)

30 days of PTO

Flexible remote days

Flexible working hours

Equity participation from day 1

Entitlement to work computer (choice of equipment)

A one-time 1,000€ remote budget to use on coworking, office setup, etc.

If you join us as an independent contractor, you’re only entitled to the first five benefits of this list.

Compensation & Package

Salary Range: 90K -130K EUR

Plus:

Interview Process

1.Recruiter / HR Call

2.Hiring Manager Interview

3.Take Home Test

4.Technical Interview

5.Group Interview

About LI.FI

Berlin-based LI.FI is providing a bridge between different blockchains, enabling developers to navigate a wide range of decentralised finance (DeFi) applications across multiple chains. The platform provides an application programme interface (API) that developers can build on to create prototypes and go to market faster.

The arrival of LI.FI comes at a time when DeFi has reemerged as a trending topic in the world of crypto, after the collapse of the centralised crypto exchange FTX. The resurgence of DeFi seems to provide a far more sustainable, scalable, and transparent crypto economy.

Backed by crypto-native investment firms CoinFund and Superscrypt, LI.FI is now in a prime position to execute its long term strategy of bringing more users to the world of Web3, by enabling tokens and orders to trade across any chain seamlessly.

LI.FI is simplifying multi-chain DeFi for traditional financial institutions, fintechs and service providers by offering a unified API to access all on-chain liquidity across major blockchains for best price same-chain and cross-chain swaps.

Mission: to enable a fairer, more decentralised world. Apart from the tech, they care a lot about user experience and aim to make complex technologies accessible to everyone.

Funding:

In December 2025 they announced the close of a $29 million Series A extension funding round. This latest round, led by Multicoin and CoinFund, brings total capital raised to $51.7 million.

They will hire from EMEA (remote).

Resources

Funding round

https://li.fi/knowledge-hub/li-fi-secures-usd29m-in-series-a-extension-led-by-multicoin-and-coinfund-to

jobsdetail.benefits:
Benefits

  • Competitive compensation package
  • Opportunity to work with cutting-edge blockchain technology
  • Collaborative environment with highly skilled team members
  • Flexible work arrangements
  • Professional development opportunities

This role is perfect for someone who is passionate about DeFi, thrives on solving complex mathematical problems at the intersection of TradFi and crypto, and wants to architect the financial engine of on-chain RWA trading.

About company

About Ostium Labs

Ostium is the first perpetuals DEX engineered specifically for Real World Assets. We build on the massive growth in on-chain perpetuals, bringing a first-of-its-kind twist through a focus on RWAs. Ostium will enable trading of an entirely new set of assets, previously unavailable on-chain — from gold & copper to wheat, oil, & forex pairs — to the most popular product in DeFi. How? Through trade execution at prices fed in via Chainlink’s new Functions product, a semi self-service oracle enabling low-latency feeds for long tail assets. We’re on track to be the first project to launch on Mainnet using Functions for settlement. A collective liquidity pool takes on counterparty risk during periods of open interest imbalance. We won the Pyth prize at xHack in October, were incubated by DeFi Alliance, and just raised 3.5M in seed funding. Our team is led by Harvard grads with prior experience at Bridgewater, IOI, IMO, and IPhO medalists, and lead engineers for Maple Finance and 21Shares. Benefits 2-4 annual company retreats (Europe & US), flexible hours, option for fully remote work.

Useful Company Links:

  • https://www.ostium.io/
  • https://twitter.com/OstiumLabs
  • https://medium.com/@ostiumlabs
  • https://tech.eu/2023/10/09/ostium-labs-35m-funding-fuels-launch-of-real-world-asset-trading-hub/

comments.text:
Hiring Criteria
Advanced background in Mathematics, Statistics, or Financial Engineering, with strong proficiency in Python (pandas, numpy, scipy).

Deep understanding of both Traditional Finance derivatives (Futures pricing, options theory) AND DeFi primitives (AMMs, Perpetual DEXs).

Proven track record of building or auditing risk engines, margin models, or liquidation systems in HFT or DeFi.

In-depth knowledge of the economics between liquidity providers and takers, including the relationship between inventory risk, spreads, volatility, and credit.

Understanding of smart contract constraints (Solidity/EVM) to ensure your mathematical models are implementable on-chain

title: Senior Web3 Software Engineer Fuel Labs

jobsdetail.requirement:
Requirements

  • 5+ years of professional experience as a software engineer, with 2+ years in a senior/lead capacity building Web3 or blockchain applications.
  • Deep expertise in modern frontend frameworks (React, Next.js, Vue) with TypeScript/JavaScript, plus Web3 libraries (ethers.js, viem, wagmi).
  • Strong experience with complex, data-intensive dapps handling real-time streams (e.g., order books, AMM curves).
  • Proven blockchain integration skills: Browser wallet connections and signing & on-chain interactions via providers (Alchemy, Infura) and libraries for reads/writes/calls.
  • Solid grasp of web3 security (private key mgmt, blind signing risks, phishing, sandwich attacks).
  • Backend experience with node services (e.g., Express, FastAPI), off-chain indexers (The Graph), and event listening.
  • Proficiency in REST/GraphQL, data fetching (SWR, TanStack Query), and state management (Zustand, Redux) in volatile crypto contexts.
  • Excellent collaboration in fast-paced, autonomous teams using GitHub, Linear, and Discord/Slack.

Nice to have

  • Experience at a CEX/DEX (e.g., Uniswap, Fuel's O2, KuCoin integrations).
  • Building trading UIs (order books, candlestick charts via TradingView/Lightweight Charts, positions, PnL calculators).
  • Performance tuning for high-throughput DeFi (sub-ms latency, WebSockets for orderflow).
  • Prior DeFi/fintech work with tokenomics, liquidity mining, or audited protocols.
  • Design systems (e.g., RainbowKit, Wagmi connectors) and tooling (Vite, esbuild, Foundry for E2E tests).

jobsdetail.description:
Description

Alliance is the leading accelerator for crypto founders. Since 2020 we’ve backed 300+ startups (Pump, Synthetix, Pendle, Believe, and many more), now collectively valued at $10B+.

We’re hiring a Software Engineer to join our in-house engineering team. You’ll report directly to  (CTO) and will be responsible for owning features from the requirements definition stage to production.

What you’ll do

  • Own products end-to-end: discovery → definition → design/UX → implementation → launch → iteration.
  • Write production-grade TypeScript across the stack (React/Next.js on the front-end; Node.js on the back-end) while following best practices.
  • Build and maintain internal tools that make our team faster.
  • Ship features across our external sites and webapps, including Space – our founder portal – and public marketing surfaces.
  • Craft polished UIs with React and our internal component library; contribute components and patterns back to the library.
  • Integrate third-party APIs (auth, payments, email) and own data models, caching, and performance.
  • Raise the bar on engineering quality: code reviews, CI/CD, reliability, and accessibility.
jobsdetail.requirement:
What we’re looking for

  • Product-minded builder who can turn ambiguous problems into shipped software without a dedicated PM or designer.
  • Deep experience with TypeScript, React, and Node.js; familiarity with Next.js and a modern database (e.g., Postgres/Prisma) is a plus.
  • Taste for UX/UI and the ability to sketch flows or lightweight wireframes before coding.
  • Hands-on IC orientation with a strong ownership bias: you set priorities, move fast, and sweat details.
  • Extremely high-agency, entrepreneurial, self-driven.
  • Comfortable using modern AI tools to move faster.
  • NYC-based or willing to relocate (non-negotiable).

Examples of strong qualifications (good to have but not required)

  • Standout GitHub: notable open-source projects, meaningful contributions, or traction.
  • Design-engineering portfolio showing polished UI work, component systems, and attention to accessibility/performance.
  • Founder or early team member of a VC-backed startup that raised millions in funding.
  • SWE at Meta, Stripe, OpenAI, or another category-defining tech company.
  • Clear signals of exceptional ability: competitive gaming/esports (chess, MtG, StarCraft), Math/Physics Olympiad medalist, top academic background (Harvard/Stanford/MIT), professional sports athlete, etc.

jobsdetail.benefits:
Agora operates a centralized USD-pegged stablecoin platform with robust compliance, security, and governance. We are equally committed to fostering a diverse, inclusive, and equitable workplace.

We are an Equal Employment Opportunity Employer. We do not discriminate based on race, color, ancestry, national origin, religion or creed, mental or physical disability, medical condition, genetic information, sex (including pregnancy, childbirth, and related conditions), gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, age, marital status, military or veteran status, citizenship, or any other characteristic protected under applicable federal, state, or local law.

Interview Process

1.Recruiter / HR Call

This initial step, candidates will need to respond to a set of questions via video recording.

jobsdetail.description:
1 HC – Germany

Location: Remote (based in Germany )

Work Model: 100% Remote

Location: Remote (Based in Germany)

Salary: Approx. 2 million RMB/year

(60%–70% base + 30%–40% performance-based commission & bonus) – Negotiable & open

Contract Type: Local third-party agency contract (until in-country entity is established)

Work Model: 100% Remote

Company Overview:

Our client is a rapidly growing global e-commerce brand specializing in professional tools and equipment. With operations in over 200 countries and a strong base of satisfied customers, the company is committed to offering high-quality products at competitive prices. Their international expansion strategy focuses on building localized teams and sales infrastructures to better serve customers across key global markets.

As part of their strategic growth in Europe, our client is seeking an entrepreneurial and results-driven Sales Director to lead market operations in Germany.

About the Role

The Sales Director (Country Manager) will be responsible for building and scaling the offline sales and channel ecosystem in their assigned country.

This is a high-autonomy, entrepreneurial role, ideal for candidates who want to:

  • Build a market from scratch
  • Own strategy, execution, and results
  • Grow into a General Manager position as the business scales

The role works closely with Global HQ (China) while leading all local market operations.

Key Responsibilities

  • Develop and execute the offline sales and channel strategy for Germany .
  • Build and manage dealer, distributor, and reseller networks
  • Drive B2C channel development through offline and hybrid models
  • Define and implement go-to-market, pricing, and brand positioning strategies
  • Establish sales targets, budgets, KPIs, and performance tracking
  • Recruit, train, and manage a local sales team
  • Maintain strong relationships with key local partners and stakeholders
  • Conduct market research and competitor analysis
  • Collect customer and channel feedback to support product optimization
  • Coordinate closely with HQ teams (product, supply chain, logistics, marketing)

title: Head of B2B Sales & Business Development (Web3 / DeFi)

jobsdetail.description:
Who We Are

Baton Corporation is the development company behind pump.fun, the dominant memecoin launchpad on Solana, processing $750M+ in daily trading volume. Over the last 2 years, pump.fun generated $950M+ in revenue with a team of fewer than 100 people.

We operate at real scale. The systems we build are used by hundreds of thousands of users, and the changes we ship reach production daily—sometimes within hours.

How We Work

  • We ship to production every day
  • We keep bureaucracy low and ownership high
  • Engineers own their platforms end-to-end
  • Decisions are data-driven and guided by experimentation

The Role

We're looking for a Senior Payments Platforms Engineer to own and scale the infrastructure backbone that powers our fiat <-> crypto payment flows. This is not a pure DevOps or SRE role — you'll be a senior technical contributor who designs cloud-native platforms, defines engineering standards, and directly enables the product and engineering teams shipping the next generation of our payments infrastructure.

You'll bring deep experience from a neobank, payment service provider, or financial institution — somewhere like Monzo, GoCardless, Wise, Checkout.com, Adyen, Stripe, Revolut, or a tier-1 bank's payments division — where you've had to make cloud infrastructure reliable, scalable, and compliant under real-world financial constraints.

What You'll Build & Own

As a Payments Platforms Engineer, you will design, build, and operate the cloud and infrastructure layer that underpins our entire payments stack. You'll work closely with our backend payments engineers, compliance team, and security function to ensure our systems are resilient, auditable, and production-grade.

You will:

  • Architect and operate our AWS-based payments infrastructure — multi-region, highly available, and designed with financial-grade resilience (active-active or active-passive failover, RTO/RPO commitments, disaster recovery runbooks).
  • Own our Infrastructure as Code (IaC) estate using Terraform, Pulumi, or CDK — full lifecycle management of AWS resources including VPCs, EKS/ECS clusters, RDS/Aurora, ElastiCache, SQS/SNS/EventBridge, and API Gateway.
  • Design and enforce network security architecture for a regulated payments environment: private subnets, VPC peering/PrivateLink for third-party integrations (Plaid, Stripe, Chainalysis, etc.), WAF rules, Shield Advanced, and robust ingress/egress controls.
  • Build and maintain CI/CD pipelines that support safe, high-frequency production deployments across our payments services — including blue/green and canary releases, automated rollback, and deployment gating tied to SLO compliance.
  • Define and operate our secrets and key management strategy — AWS KMS, Secrets Manager, HSM integration where required for PCI DSS scope, and robust secret rotation practices.
  • Own observability infrastructure — centralised logging (CloudWatch, OpenSearch or equivalent), distributed tracing (AWS X-Ray or OpenTelemetry), metrics pipelines, and alerting that surfaces real payment failures, latency spikes, and compliance-relevant anomalies to on-call engineers.
  • Platform compliance and audit readiness — design infrastructure controls that satisfy PCI DSS, SOC 2, and relevant FCA/regulatory expectations: immutable audit logs, access controls, data residency, encryption at rest and in transit, and evidence generation for auditors.
  • Manage data platform infrastructure for our payments data — RDS Aurora PostgreSQL (primary OLTP, event streaming MSK/Kafka or Kinesis), and integration with any analytical stores used for reconciliation and reporting.
  • Build reliability tooling — SLO/SLA dashboards, automated runbooks, chaos engineering experiments, and load/stress test harnesses for critical payment paths.
  • Own disaster recovery and backup strategy — design and test multi-region failover, define RTO/RPO targets per service tier, build automated backup pipelines, and maintain runbooks for full and partial recovery scenarios. Ensure business continuity is provable, not assumed.
  • Drive platform cost engineering — rightsizing, Reserved/Savings Plans, Spot strategy for non-payment-critical workloads, and tagging taxonomy for financial allocation across products.
  • Support and mentor backend engineers on platform best practices — container packaging, 12-factor apps, environment parity, and safe use of managed AWS services.
